title: Renewal: BluePeak Private - add regional standby clusters & term alignment
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Olivia Bennett <olivia.bennett@bluepeakmed.com>, BluePeak Procurement <procurement@bluepeakmed.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Sun, 1 Jul 2028 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: BluePeak private renewal & standby cluster request

Hi Olivia / procurement team,

Quick note as we start planning the July renewal window for your Private deployment: BluePeak indicated you'd like to add two regional standby clusters (US‑EAST and US‑WEST) plus an isolated analytics cluster for the research team. We want to make sure we capture the following so our SOW and pricing are accurate:

- Desired RTO for the standby clusters (minutes/hours)
- Expected sustained qps and token throughput per cluster (p95)
- Whether the analytics cluster needs strict tenant isolation (VPC peering restricted) or full network airgap
- Any longer audit/log retention or data residency constraints beyond standard 2 years

I've put a short call on the table next week to walk through technical sizing. If you can share rough throughput numbers and preferred renewal date alignment (you previously mentioned Oct 1 as a target), we'll draft an updated SOW.

Draft artifacts to follow — topology diagram + initial SOW for review.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
---
Attachment stub: topology_diagram.png (PNG, 420 KB)


From: Olivia Bennett <olivia.bennett@bluepeakmed.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: BluePeak Procurement <procurement@bluepeakmed.com>, BluePeak IT Ops <itops@bluepeakmed.com>
Date: Mon, 2 Jul 2028 11:02:00 -0400
Subject: Re: BluePeak private renewal & standby cluster request

Hi Ava — thanks for the heads up.

High level from our side:
- Targeting Oct 1 renewal alignment for all private environments (good to have one cutover date)
- Standby clusters: 2 additional (one in us‑east, one in us‑west) intended for DR with < 15 minute RTO; primary traffic stays in us‑central
- Analytics cluster: yes, full network isolation preferred (no peering to our primary VPCs)
- Expected throughput for failover: ~120 qps sustained for each standby, spikes to 400 qps for short windows
- Audit retention: likely need 7 years for some clinical audit trails (will confirm exact records)

Attaching our internal requirements worksheet. Also: procurement will want a line item for a 90‑day pilot for the analytics cluster before full commit.

Olivia
Director, Infrastructure
BluePeak Medical
---
Attachment stub: BluePeak_requirements.xlsx (XLSX, 95 KB)


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Olivia Bennett <olivia.bennett@bluepeakmed.com>, BluePeak Procurement <procurement@bluepeakmed.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 3 Jul 2028 08:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: BluePeak private renewal & standby cluster request

Hi Olivia — this is really helpful, thanks.

Summary of what we'll include in the SOW/quote based on your notes:
- Two DR standby clusters (us‑east, us‑west) sized for 120 qps sustained / 400 qps peak
- One isolated analytics cluster with a 90‑day pilot line item (option to convert to committed within renewal term)
- Audit/log retention addendum for 7 years (will require minor storage delta)
- Renewal alignment to Oct 1 across all active private sites

I've attached a draft SOW and a simple topology diagram for review. Rough order of magnitude pricing is included in the SOW header — it's preliminary until Ben runs final commercial terms.

Can we do a 30‑minute call Thu 5th at 10am ET to walk pricing and pilot terms? If that's tight, suggest Fri morning.

Ava Chen
Enterprise Sales, Redwood Inference
Phone: +1 (415) 555‑0123
---
Attachment stub: SOW_BluePeak_2028_draft.pdf (PDF, 210 KB)
Attachment stub: topology_diagram.png (PNG, 420 KB)

On Mon, Jul 2, 2028 at 11:02 AM Olivia Bennett <olivia.bennett@bluepeakmed.com> wrote:
> Hi Ava — thanks for the heads up.
> 
> High level from our side: ...


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: BluePeak Procurement <procurement@bluepeakmed.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 5 Jul 2028 13:05:00 -0400
Subject: Pricing & commercial options — BluePeak standby clusters + analytics pilot (D-89432-BP)

Hi Olivia / procurement team,

Following Ava's SOW draft, here's the commercial summary for internal review:

- Two standby clusters (us‑east / us‑west): $48,000/month (combined) at list; 12% discount if contracted as a 12‑month committed addendum
- Isolated analytics cluster (90‑day pilot): one‑time pilot fee $14,500, conversion to committed capacity thereafter at $18,000/month
- Audit/log retention 7 years: estimated additional $1,200/month (storage & retention indexing)
- If BluePeak consolidates renewal date to Oct 1 for all sites, we can offer a one‑time onboarding credit of $8,000 to offset migration sequencing costs

Please confirm the billing contact and whether BluePeak would like the pilot billed as prepaid or invoice‑upon‑conversion. Once procurement confirms, I'll prepare a pricing addendum and updated price sheet.

Ben Carter
Head of Sales — Enterprise
Redwood Inference
---
Attachment stub: price_sheet_bp_v2.xlsx (XLSX, 85 KB)

On Tue, Jul 3, 2028 at 08:30 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Hi Olivia — this is really helpful, thanks.
> 
> Summary of what we'll include in the SOW/quote based on your notes:...


From: Olivia Bennett <olivia.bennett@bluepeakmed.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: BluePeak Procurement <procurement@bluepeakmed.com>, BluePeak IT Ops <itops@bluepeakmed.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Jul 2028 19:45:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Pricing & commercial options — BluePeak standby clusters + analytics pilot (D-89432-BP)

Ben / Ava — appreciate the clear options. Quick update from procurement/legal:

- Pricing looks acceptable in principle; procurement prefers pilot billed as prepaid (covers license + onboarding)
- Please include a 7‑year audit retention addendum in the final docs — compliance will need that in the contract
- Legal will want a short clause on data residency for the analytics cluster (we need a written guarantee it will remain in US regions and not be replicated outside the agreed regions)
- We accept the Oct 1 alignment and the onboarding credit offer — can you prepare an addendum that folds the standby clusters + pilot into the existing renewal instead of a standalone PO?

Next steps requested by BluePeak:
1) Ben to prepare an updated price addendum and send via DocuSign for procurement review (please cc our legal: legal@bluepeakmed.com)
2) Ava / Karthik to confirm expected cutover runbook and estimated blackout windows for the Oct 1 migration

Once we have the DocuSign link and addendum, procurement will prioritize routing. Thanks — looking forward to closing this quickly.

Olivia Bennett
Director, Infrastructure
BluePeak Medical
---

title: Request: Extended Research Credits + Governance Docs for CivicData Hub Pilot
preview:
From: Dr. Anna R. Patel <anna@civicdata.org>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Cc: CivicData Hub Legal <legal@civicdata.org>
Date: Mon, 02 Jul 2028 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot credits request and governance docs

Hi Alex,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the Redwood runtime looks promising for our transit anonymization pipeline. CivicData Hub runs a volunteer-driven pilot with three mid-size cities; we want to run a 3-month analysis of ridership patterns and anonymized origin-destination matrices.

Two asks:
- Can Redwood extend trial credits for the pilot beyond the standard 30 days? We're estimating ~1M tokens for the 3-month run.
- We need governance documentation (SOC2 attestation or equivalent, data handling and retention policy) to satisfy our board and city legal teams.

Attached is a short SOW and a one-page pilot description. If you need a formal Letter of Intent I can share that too.

Best,
Anna
Dr. Anna R. Patel
Research Director, CivicData Hub
anna@civicdata.org | +1 415-555-0189
[attachment: CivicData_SOW.docx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document, 48KB)]

From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Dr. Anna R. Patel <anna@civicdata.org>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 03 Jul 2028 10:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot credits request and governance docs

Hi Anna — great to meet last week, and thanks for the SOW.

Short answer: we can support a pilot beyond the standard 30 days, but I need to confirm the credit amount with our promotions and partnerships teams. Quick plan: 
- I can pre-approve a 30-day extension + 500k token credit from our pilot pool.
- For the full 3-month, 1M token ask, I'll escalate for an additional credit grant and coordinate with Marissa and Ben.
- I attached the basic SOC2 readiness letter we can share while you complete vendor paperwork. For anything formal your legal team needs, we can provide the SOC2 PDF and a short data retention attestation.

Can you confirm the start date you want for the pilot and the primary contact for DPA/legal discussions? Also: any constraints on bring-your-own-encryption or region?

I'll loop Ben and Marissa for approval.

Thanks,
Alex Martinez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
alex@redwood.com | +1 650-555-0123
[attachment: Redwood_SOC2_prelim_letter.pdf (application/pdf, 58KB)]

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 04 Jul 2028 09:18:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot credits request and governance docs

Alex / Marissa —

Looping in here. Quick details I pulled from HubSpot (RDW-DEAL-2028-118): CivicData Hub is a non-profit consortium, low ACV; pilot could convert to a paid contract in Q4 if the cities commit. Given the public-sector risk profiles, approving an accelerated credit grant (1M tokens) is ok from a GTM POV but needs a signed MOA that includes an explicit data handling attestation and a limited usage clause.

Ask: Marissa, can partnerships approve 1M tokens + 90-day extension if we require MOA + SOC2 attestation and limit credits to the pilot org (no redistribution)? If yes I will tag this in the promotions queue and notify finance for negative budget impact (low).

Ben
Director, Growth — Redwood Inference

From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 05 Jul 2028 11:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot credits request and governance docs

Ben / Alex —

Approve as requested with conditions. Approve 1,000,000 tokens and a 90-day extension contingent on:
1) Signed MOA (limited to CivicData Hub pilot entities)
2) Receipt of a vendor questionnaire / SOC2 letter on file (we can accept an attestation from your auditor if SOC2 report is in-process)
3) Quarterly check-in and a short usage report after 45 days

Alex — please prepare the MOA and attach the SOC2 letter we already provided. I'll sign off and request final finance tagging. I'll also ask Legal to confirm the DPA language if their legal team proposes edits (cc Legal on the outbound).

Marissa Cole
Head of Partnerships, Redwood Inference

From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Dr. Anna R. Patel <anna@civicdata.org>
Cc: CivicData Hub Legal <legal@civicdata.org>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 09 Jul 2028 11:08:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot credits request and governance docs

Anna — good news. Per our internal approvals, Redwood will provide the following for the CivicData Hub transit pilot:

- 1,000,000 free token credits (applied to pilot account)
- Pilot window: 90 days from the agreed start date
- Conditions: signed MOA limiting use to the named pilot participants, and SOC2 attestation or equivalent on file.

Attached: Pilot_MOA.pdf (please review, sign via DocuSign), SOC2_letter.pdf (Redwood preliminary attestation), and a templated SOW (CivicData_SOW.docx) populated with the pilot cities and timelines.

Next steps:
1) Review MOA and sign via DocuSign: https://www.docusign.com/en/example/rdw-civicdata-moa
2) Send the signed MOA + vendor questionnaire to legal@redwood.com
3) Confirm desired pilot start date (we can backdate credit activation to the first data ingest date if needed)

If your legal wants edits to the DPA, please flag them and cc Marissa — we'll route any redlines to our Legal AG.

Thanks — excited to get this pilot running.

—Alex
Alex Martinez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
alex@redwood.com | +1 650-555-0123
[attachment: Pilot_MOA.pdf (application/pdf, 142KB)]

title: OzoneAI private offer — tiered SKU packaging & partner uplift proposal
preview:
From: Samir Patel <samir.patel@ozoneai.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Cc: procurement@ozoneai.com
Date: Fri, 5 Jul 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Packaging question for private offer — reseller uplift

Hi Camila,

Thanks for the demo yesterday. Procurement here is trying to reconcile two asks before we move the PO to signature: 1) whether Redwood can split the private-offer SKUs into a base compute SKU + optional feature add-ons (usage-based) so our reseller can invoice cleanly, and 2) what channel margin would look like for our reseller (CloudReseller).

Specifically:
- Ozone wants a flat monthly commitment for predicted tokens (\"Base\") and separate metered SKUs for high-cost features (fine-tuning, private indexing). Our reseller needs at least a 14% net margin to justify marketing and lead routing.
- Do you support a tiered uplift on the channel side if we hit higher commits? E.g., 14% at baseline, 18% after 12-month commit target hit.

Can you share a sample packaging and a high-level margin workback? We're on a tight timeline — hope to finalize commercial terms this week.

Best,
Samir
Samir Patel | Head of Procurement, Ozone AI
procurement@ozoneai.com

---
Attachments: none


From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Samir Patel <samir.patel@ozoneai.com>
Cc: procurement@ozoneai.com, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 5 Jul 2028 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Packaging question for private offer — reseller uplift

Hi Samir — great questions and thanks for the follow-up. High level answers first, details below and attaching a draft PO packaging and a margin workback.

Short answers:
- Yes — we can present the private offer as a Base commitment SKU (flat monthly commit) plus metered add-ons for higher-cost capabilities. That maps cleanly to CloudReseller invoicing models.
- We can support a tiered partner uplift: baseline commission for the reseller, with an automated uplift applied retroactively if the customer exceeds predefined commit thresholds. We typically model 12% baseline with an uplift band up to 18% depending on committed ARR and certification cadence.

Implementation notes (packaging):
- Base SKU: \"redwood-inference.base-commit\" — prepay monthly/annual with token floor and overage metering.
- Feature add-ons (metered): fine-tuning, private index calls, and priority inference (separate meter IDs).
- Invoice path: Redwood will invoice Ozone; reseller gets a calculated pass-through uplift paid as a partner rebate on a monthly settlement.

Margin workback (attached): I included a simple model showing how a 12% baseline can become 18% when a 3x usage uplift and 12-month commit are met. Ben (cc) — can you confirm the acceptable minimum margin for Redwood in a reseller routing scenario and any cap we should surface?

We can also produce an alternative where Redwood invoices the reseller directly (reseller-managed commercial flow) if that helps procurement.

Hi Samir — looping in CloudReseller (Liu) to confirm the routing preference. Per Ben, our recommended, finance-friendly structure is below — please confirm which routing you'd prefer and whether a 12% baseline / 15% / 18% uplift band is acceptable to Ozone:

Recommended (fastest sign): Redwood invoices Ozone; Redwood pays partner uplift as monthly settlement (partner rebate). Pros: faster to onboard (1 week), single tax/invoice flow. Cons: partner receives rebate rather than direct invoice line-item.

Alternative (reseller-led): Redwood invoices CloudReseller; reseller invoices Ozone and keeps a margin. Pros: better reseller visibility and a cleaner commercial relationship for your channel motions. Cons: 2–3 week reseller onboarding, changes to invoicing/tax flow.

Proposed commercial terms (high level) to insert into private-offer term sheet:

- Metered add-ons: billed monthly based on consumed units for fine-tuning, private indexing, and priority inference.
- Partner uplift bands: 12% baseline; 15% when rolling 3-month consumption > 1.5x baseline; 18% when annual committed tokens >= target X. Retroactive uplift applies on the months that triggered the band, with settlement within 30 days of month close.
- Minimum partner net margin: 10% (Redwood will not route partners if net falls below 10% unless commercial exceptions approved by Sales Ops).

Next steps: Samir / procurement — confirm routing preference and whether Ozone wants the 12% baseline or a raised 14% baseline (we can offset list price modestly). If you confirm routing + baseline, we'll deliver a redline-ready term sheet and updated PO draft by EOD tomorrow.

title: BlueHarbor: intermittent live-response interruptions causing error/latency spikes — exec sync requested
preview:
From: Julia Su <julia.su@blueharbor.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-07-05T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Live responses: repeated stalls and 504s during demo window

Ben —

We first saw this during yesterday's 10:00 ET demo. Several user sessions showed long pauses (10–20s) where the streaming output stopped entirely, followed by a 504 on the client. Error rate for live responses jumped from ~0.5% to 6% over the hour; p50 latency also increased by ~80ms. Attachments: bh_logs_20280705_bundle.zip (log bundle), screenshot-504-0725.png.

Steps we've observed:
- Client opens websocket and begins token stream. Tokens arrive for 3–8s, then stream is silent for 10–20s.
- After the silent window client receives a single 504 or an SSE reconnect.
- The behavior is intermittent; happens across two regions (us-east, us-west) but more frequent in us-east.

We captured full HARs and a log bundle — attached. This is customer-facing and their CTO is asking for an ETA. Can you advise next steps and who we should loop in on your side?

Thanks,
Julia Su
Senior Platform Engineer, BlueHarbor
julia.su@blueharbor.com



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Julia Su <julia.su@blueharbor.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-07-05T09:40:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Live responses: repeated stalls and 504s during demo window

Thanks Julia — really appreciate the quick capture and the logs.

Monica / David — looping you in. BlueHarbor (big demo this morning) is seeing intermittent streaming pauses leading to 504s and a visible latency/error spike. They've attached logs and a screenshot.

Immediate asks:
- Monica: can CS open a customer-facing incident and confirm the exact demo timeframe and number of affected sessions?
- David: can SRE triage the logs for proxy timeouts, chunked transfer stalls, and any upstream model throttling?

Julia — we'll get back with an ETA in ~60–90 minutes. If you have a PagerDuty incident id or a direct contact, send it over.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai
Cell: +1 (415) 555-0192



From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Julia Su <julia.su@blueharbor.com>
Cc: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-07-05T10:15:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Live responses: repeated stalls and 504s during demo window

Julia — I've opened INC-9248 on our side and have product and support notes attached to the record. Ben — I confirmed the window: 2028-07-04 10:00–11:00 ET, roughly 48 concurrent demo sessions, 7 of which reported visible interruptions.

For BlueHarbor: could you also share the exact client SDK version and any proxy/load-balancer siting in front of your browsers? Those details help us reproduce.

I'll be the customer point-of-contact to provide updates and coordinate timely communication.

Regards,
Monica Patel
Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
monica.patel@redwood.ai



From: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Julia Su <julia.su@blueharbor.com>
Date: 2028-07-05T11:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Live responses: repeated stalls and 504s during demo window

Julia / team — quick triage notes after an initial pass over the logs you sent: bh_logs_20280705_bundle.zip.

What I see so far:
- A pattern of delayed chunk delivery from our edge proxy around the times you flagged; client-level TCP shows the WS connection is open but application-level frames are not forwarded for windows of ~8–18s.
- Proxy logs show intermittent upstream 502/timeout responses from the model-serving cluster at those same timestamps.
- No consistent OOM or GPU throttling indicators in the model host metrics. CPU and GPU counters look nominal.

Hypothesis: some upstream host is intermittently hitting a transient blocking condition while holding the connection open but not emitting tokens (e.g., momentary event-loop stall or RPC backpressure). That would look like 'open socket, no tokens'.

Next steps I'm requesting:
1) BlueHarbor: can you drop a pcap or full TCP dump for one affected session to confirm whether the pause is network-level or application-level?
2) We'll spin a canary configuration that reduces batch size and disables cross-request batching for a couple minutes in the affected region to see if throughput/backpressure improves.

I'll post realtime findings to INC-9248 and a PD link if we need escalation.

—David
David Kim
Senior SRE, Redwood Inference
david.kim@redwood.ai



From: Julia Su <julia.su@blueharbor.com>
To: David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ethan Lowe <ethan.lowe@blueharbor.com>
Date: 2028-07-05T12:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Live responses: repeated stalls and 504s during demo window

Thanks for the quick investigation, David.

We've uploaded a PCAP and additional HARs to the Drive folder (link below). In the PCAP for session id bh-demo-47 I can confirm TCP kept the socket established while no application frames were exchanged for ~12s.

PagerDuty: PD-9248 (we also opened an internal incident). Our CTO is on the chain and asks whether this is something you can fix on your side (i.e., configuration/fallback) or if we need to adjust our client SDK behavior. They need a rough ETA to decide whether to continue the afternoon demos.

Drive (logs): https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-BH-logs
PD link: https://pagerduty.com/incidents/PD-9248

Julia Su
Senior Platform Engineer, BlueHarbor



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Julia Su <julia.su@blueharbor.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, David Kim <david.kim@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-07-06T16:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: BlueHarbor live interruptions — exec sync & proposed mitigation

Julia / Ethan —

Appreciate the patience. Quick exec-level update and concrete mitigation options from our side:

What we have so far (summary):
- Pattern confirmed: application-level "silent windows" while WS kept open; correlated with intermittent upstream 502/timeouts from a small subset of hosts in us-east.
- Hypothesis: transient RPC backpressure on specific model-serving instances causing them to hold connections without emitting tokens.

Immediate mitigation options we can deploy in the next 30–45 minutes:
1) Canary: route BlueHarbor traffic in us-east to a reduced-batch, lower-concurrency fleet (no batching) as a rapid rollback to reduce chance of backpressure. Expected effect: reduces error spike and silent windows for likely degraded hosts.
2) Client fallback: add client-side token timeout (5s) with automatic retry to a secondary region — requires a small SDK patch on your side and would be ~2–4 hours to ship.
3) Permanent: we will identify the affected hosts and drain them for deeper kernel/event-loop analysis; full remediation ETA: 48–72 hours depending on root cause.

We recommend option (1) immediately to stabilize demos. Monica will coordinate messaging. Rishi — wanted to make you aware in case BlueHarbor escalates to execs; we may need an SLA credit conversation if this materially impacted their event.

Julia — please confirm if you want us to flip the canary now. If yes, we will:
- deploy the reduced-batching route for BlueHarbor in us-east, monitor PD-9248, and share a 30-min check-in.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai
Office: +1 (415) 555-0101

preview:
From: Sarah Tan <sarah.tan@aperturemfg.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 05 Jul 2028 16:12:00 -0400
Subject: Re: Onboarding - portal access and PO visibility

Hi Ben,

We completed the Coupa invite flow this morning, but our finance team can't see new POs in the supplier portal. The UI shows us as 'logged in' (we get a successful SSO redirect) but the PO list is empty. If we try a second login immediately, we get routed into a different tenant view with a different org name in the header — seems like session affinity/page sharding.

Attached are: a short trace of the SSO redirect (sso-redirect-trace.txt), and a CSV export of the pending POs (pending-po-list.csv) with the PO numbers that are invisible. We also uploaded our W9 (w9-scan.pdf) during onboarding.

This is blocking our AP approval cycle; three POs (PO-9482, PO-9483, PO-9489) need urgent acknowledgement to avoid payment delays. Can you check if there is a tenant-mapping or cookie partitioning issue on your side?

Thanks,
Sarah Tan
Accounts Payable Lead

From: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sarah Tan <sarah.tan@aperturemfg.com>
Date: Wed, 05 Jul 2028 18:01:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Onboarding - portal access and PO visibility

Ben / Marissa —

I pulled logs for the Aperture redirect flow and confirmed two Set-Cookie headers being emitted during the auth handoff from our redirector: one cookie for _rwd_shard and another for _rwd_affinity. Chrome's partitioned cookie behavior can treat those as separate contexts when third-party protections are enabled, which would explain the UI switching between tenant views on subsequent hits.

Immediate mitigation I tried from ops: added a short-lived sticky cookie (ttl=30s) on the redirect endpoint to bias the flow to a single shard. I deployed the change to the staging redirector and replicated the flow with a simulated customer browser: PO list appears consistently.

Next steps:
- Promote sticky cookie change to production after a quick canary (I scheduled SUP-4821-canary for 19:00 UTC tonight).
- Marissa, if the W9 upload landed in a different path because the profile was created under the alternate shard, we may need to rebind the profile objects to the correct tenant id (small DB migration).
- Ben, after the canary I'll ask support to re-invite Aperture with a fresh link that forces a new session.

Sarah, if you can try the incognito test and reply with results that will speed validation; otherwise we will push the change and send you a validated link.

-Jordan

title: Order Form: invoicing split & Net terms exception
preview:
From: Elena Morales <elena.morales@helixstream.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-07-01T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Order Form: request for invoicing split & Net30 exception

Hi Ben —

Thanks again for the demo last week. Procurement asked us to get the order form in place this month, but we have two quick asks before we can issue a PO: 1) invoice needs to be split between HelixStream US (taxable) and HelixStream Global Ltd (EU entity) for their respective spend lines, and 2) they require Net 30 terms but procurement is trying to negotiate a Net 45 exception on the commitment payments with their AP team.

I attached our initial PO and the draft order form (HelixStream_OrderForm_v3_request.pdf). Can Redwood confirm whether you can:
- Issue two invoices tied to the same order form but different remit entities, and
- Accept Net 45 on the activation tranche for 90 days while we complete internal credit approvals?

If this is acceptable we can skip extra legal review on our end and route the PO.

Appreciate a quick call if helpful — Josh (cc) will be our contract lead.

Best,
Elena Morales
Head of Procurement, HelixStream
elena.morales@helixstream.com
(415) 555-0198

Attachment: HelixStream_OrderForm_v3_request.pdf (application/pdf)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@helixstream.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-07-02T10:07:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Order Form: request for invoicing split & Net30 exception

Elena —

Thanks for the clear ask and for attaching the PO. Quick confirmation up front: we can support multiple invoice recipients for a single Order Form, but we need Finance + Legal to sign off on the payment flow and the Net45 exception. I’m looping in Kimberly (Finance) and Jonas (Legal) to scope the necessary redline language and tax handling. Markus from Commercial will help with the minimum commit tie-break and discount approvals.

Proposed next steps (so we’re aligned internally):
1) Finance reviews invoice-splitting operationally and confirms if separate invoices can be routed to two remit entities in our billing system (Kimberly)
2) Legal reviews Order Form redline to capture Net45 carveout for activation tranche and any indemnity for tax/residency (Jonas)
3) Commercial confirms whether the requested Net45 impacts the committed discount / milestone schedule (Markus)

Can we aim to have a redlined Order Form back to you by EOD 7/4? If procurement will sign a conditional PO, please send the PO number (or updated PO with both entity details) and any required invoice headers.

Ben
Ben Carter | Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 650-555-0102

Quoted for context below:
> On Jul 1, 2028, at 9:12 AM, Elena Morales <elena.morales@helixstream.com> wrote:
> > Thanks again for the demo last week. Procurement asked us to get the order form in place this month...

Attachments referenced: HelixStream_OrderForm_v3_request.pdf


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-07-03T11:15:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Order Form: request for invoicing split & Net30 exception

Folks —

I looked through the PO and the requested split. A few immediate operational items:
- Our billing system supports separate invoice recipients on a single order if we have separate remit-to addresses and tax IDs. We need both entities' legal names, VAT/GST numbers, and remittance addresses on file before we issue invoices.
- For US withholding/resale tax: if HelixStream Global Ltd is invoiced for EU spend, we need to capture their VAT registration to avoid withholding at source.
- Net45 exception: Finance can approve a one-time Net45 for the activation tranche, but we require an explicit term in the Order Form that references an approved exception and a counterparty PO number. Also need assurance that the Net45 does not delay recognition of revenue for Redwood; we'll require payment milestones on the invoice schedule.

Suggested Order Form language to capture the exception (draft):
"Payment Terms Exception: Notwithstanding the Payment Terms section of the MSA and Order Form, Redwood Inference agrees to accept Net 45 payment terms for the Activation Tranche only, conditioned on (i) prior receipt of a valid Purchase Order referencing this Order Form; (ii) provision of remit-to legal entity details and tax ID(s); and (iii) an invoice schedule listing the Activation Tranche as a separate line item for accounting purposes. All subsequent invoices revert to Net 30."

I also attached a small worksheet for the entity fields I need (invoice_schedule.xlsx).

If Legal confirms the language is OK we can turn around a redline for Elena.

Kimberly Park
Director, Billing & Revenue Operations
Redwood Inference
kimberly.park@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0124

Attachment: invoice_schedule.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)


From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Elena Morales <elena.morales@helixstream.com>
Date: 2028-07-04T09:58:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Order Form: request for invoicing split & Net30 exception

Ben / Kim / Elena —

Thanks for the thread. High level: Legal is comfortable with a narrowly scoped Net45 for the activation tranche so long as we: (1) limit the exception to a single invoice line tied to the activation fee, (2) include an express qualification that all other commercial terms (including indemnities, data residency clauses, and the MSA) remain unchanged, and (3) retain Redwood's right to withhold services if invoices are overdue beyond 60 days.

Proposed redline excerpt to add to the Order Form (suggested):
--- begin redline ---
Insert new paragraph under "Payment Terms":
"Activation Tranche Payment Exception: The Parties agree that the Activation Tranche identified in this Order Form may be paid on Net 45 terms provided that (a) HelixStream submits a Purchase Order that references this Order Form prior to commencement; (b) the activation fee is separately invoiced and clearly identified as a discrete line item; and (c) the invoiced amount is not in dispute. Except as set forth in this paragraph, all other payment obligations under the MSA and this Order Form shall remain subject to the Payment Terms specified therein. Redwood reserves the right to suspend delivery of Services if payment for any invoice remains unpaid more than sixty (60) days from the invoice date."
--- end redline ---

Tax note: we should add an explicit warranty from HelixStream that the remit entities have the tax registrations they provide and to indemnify Redwood for any withholding applied by tax authorities. Elena — please confirm the VAT numbers for both entities and whether HelixStream will accept vendor-side tax evidence requests.

I attached a redline PDF of the Order Form with the above language (Redwood_OrderForm_v3_redline.pdf).

Jonas Weber
Senior Counsel, Commercial Contracts
Redwood Inference
jonas.weber@redwood.com

Attachment: Redwood_OrderForm_v3_redline.pdf (application/pdf)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Elena Morales <elena.morales@helixstream.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus.klein@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-07-05T16:25:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Order Form: request for invoicing split & Net30 exception

Elena —

Thanks — looping back with the consolidated redline and the finance checklist. Summary:
- Legal has proposed narrowly scoped language to permit Net45 on the Activation Tranche only (attached).
- Finance can execute the invoice split operationally once we receive legal names, remit addresses, and VAT/GST/Tax IDs for both HelixStream entities (Kimberly attached worksheet).
- Commercial: Markus, can you confirm whether the Net45 on the activation tranche affects the committed volume discount or the milestone cadence? If it does, we need an internal approval for the concession.

Elena — if HelixStream can provide the two remit entities and VAT IDs by EOD 7/6 we will: (a) redline the Order Form as sent by Jonas, (b) create two invoice recipients in the billing system, and (c) issue an updated Order Form back to you for signature. If HelixStream can also send a conditional PO referencing the Order Form number, that will speed Finance approval.

Ben
Ben Carter | Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

> Ben / Kim / Elena —
> > Thanks for the thread. High level: Legal is comfortable with a narrowly scoped Net45 for the activation tranche so long as we...

title: Invoice sender/proxy decision + email fanout for GrowthGrid AP
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Lina Park <lina@growthgrid.com>, AP Team <ap@growthgrid.com>
Cc: procurement@growthgrid.com
Date: Wed, 04 Jul 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Invoice sender/proxy decision + options for GrowthGrid AP

Hi Lina / GrowthGrid AP team,

Thanks for the quick intro call earlier. To keep the invoice handoff low-friction for your AP stack, we can do one of two patterns:

1) Portal primary (recommended) — Redwood pushes invoices to your portal enrollment; we also emit a PDF/email copy to ap@growthgrid.com for your processing queue (fanout).
2) Email-first with signed token — we push signed PDF + XML payload directly to ap@growthgrid.com and/or a webhook to your AP system.

Default questions so we pick the right path:
- Preferred primary channel (portal vs email push)?
- Do you require UBL/UBL-2.1 / custom XML schema attachments, or will a PDF + metadata + PO suffice?
- Billing contact (name + email) and remit entity (if you have multiple ledgers)
- PO number to tie to the first invoice (please attach PO if available)
- VAT/Tax ID for invoice header

Attached is an invoice_sample-gg8892.pdf showing our usual layout and metadata fields. If you can attach PO_GF-8892.pdf in your reply that would speed onboarding.

Once you confirm, I’ll coordinate portal activation (if portal) or the signed-email flow and share expected first-delivery timestamps.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0192

Attachments: invoice_sample-gg8892.pdf


From: Lina Park <lina@growthgrid.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: AP Team <ap@growthgrid.com>, procurement@growthgrid.com
Date: Wed, 04 Jul 2028 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Invoice sender/proxy decision + options for GrowthGrid AP

Hi Ben — thanks, this looks good. A few clarifications from our side:

- GrowthGrid prefers portal enrollment if the portal can provide an API/webhook for automatic ingestion. If portal activation will take longer than 10 business days, we will accept email push until portal is live.
- Our AP system requires either (a) a PDF+CSV metadata attachment (acceptable) or (b) UBL XML. We can accept PDF-only for the first invoice but will require XML for recurring automated ingest.

title: Paxion Health DPA + SCCs: redline review and evidence timeline
preview:
From: Clara Sato <clara.sato@paxionhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@paxionhealth.com>
Date: 2028-07-01T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Paxion - DPA redline + SCCs – questions on roles and timeline

Ben — attaching our redlined DPA with SCCs appended (Paxion_DPA_Redline_v1.docx). Key asks: 

1) Role mapping: we marked a couple of clauses where Paxion expects a Processor-only relationship for telemetry and usage logs (sec. 2.1) but the redline from your counsel flags a Controller/Processor split. Can you confirm how Redwood intends to classify operational logs used for product improvement?

2) SCCs: Paxion needs confirmation that the standard EU SCCs will be incorporated and that any additional routing/hosting exceptions are handled by a Transfer Impact Addendum (TIA). We flagged a few hosting-related notes in Exhibit B.

3) Evidence & timing: our legal team requires SOC2 type II attestation (latest) and a current subprocessor inventory with onboarding dates before we can finalize procurement. Can Redwood provide an ETA for those? We can accept a rolling handoff but need a firm date.

We'd like a point-by-point redline back by EOD Friday 7/5 so procurement can align with Paxion's board review next week.

Thanks,
Clara Sato
Legal Ops, Paxion Health

Attachment: Paxion_DPA_Redline_v1.docx (docx)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2028-07-01T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: Paxion - DPA redline + SCCs – questions on roles and timeline

Laura / Jonas — forwarding Paxion's redline and asks. Quick summary and my suggested approach: 

• Role mapping (telemetry): Paxion is pushing to treat operational logs as Processor-only. Our counsel previously marked product improvement as Controller activity for model/feature telemetry. We should evaluate whether we can concede telemetry used solely for product ops (uptime, billing) but reserve Controller rights for ML training/feature analytics.

• SCCs & TIA: They want SCCs plus a TIA for hosting exceptions. We can accept SCCs; the TIA is reasonable but we need to limit the scope (only if hosting deviates from standard regions).

• Evidence: SOC2 Type II — we have the 2027 report; subprocessor inventory is exportable. ETA: I can get security to deliver the inventory by 7/3 and the SOC2 redacted copy by 7/4. Is that acceptable to Paxion?

Asks for legal: 1) Laura, propose compromise text for sec.2.1 (telemetry) and suggested SCC insertion. 2) Jonas, please call out any commercial triggers if we accept subprocessor evidence on a rolling basis (e.g., escrow/holdback language).

I'll loop back to Clara once we have consolidated redline.

— Ben
Account Exec, Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2028-07-01T12:18:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Paxion - DPA redline + SCCs – questions on roles and timeline

Ben / Jonas — reviewed Paxion's redline and flagged items below. Inline and high-level notes: 

1) Telemetry / role split (sec.2.1 / def. \"Personal Data\"): 
- Proposed language: \"Redwood will act as Processor for telemetry data that is strictly operational in nature (service health, billing, uptime). Redwood will act as Controller only where telemetry is processed to develop or improve models or features that derive product insights.\" 
- Rationale: gives us room for product analytics while conceding pure ops telemetry.

2) SCCs & TIA: 
- We can accept EU SCCs. For the Transfer Impact Addendum, counter-propose limiting scope: \"TIA applies only where Customer requests hosting outside of the agreed regions or where export is necessary due to regulatory or legal obligation.\" 
- Add a clause that any TIA engagement requires a 30-day notice and is subject to mutual agreement on mitigations.

3) Evidence requests (SOC2 / subprocessor list): 
- SOC2: redacted report is fine. We should include a standard non-waiver paragraph: \"Provision of SOC2 report does not create new warranties beyond Section X.\"
- Subprocessors: offer the current inventory with onboarding dates but add a commitment window (e.g., \"Redwood will notify Customer of new subprocessors 30 days prior to onboarding\"). If Paxion insists on immediate prior notice, we should ask for a carve-out: security-critical subprocessors can be added with retrospective notice and justification.

4) Commercial / procurement impact (Jonas): if Paxion conditions go-live on receipt of full SOC2 and signed DPA, we should ensure the order form includes standard 60-day acceptance window and limit credits/penalties tied solely to evidence delivery.

I will prepare a consolidated redline and short memo of each change by EOD 7/1. Jonas, please confirm any commercial redlines by COB.

Laura Bennett
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Clara Sato <clara.sato@paxionhealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@paxionhealth.com>
Date: 2028-07-01T16:05:00-07:00
Subject: Paxion DPA — consolidated redline and next steps

Clara — thanks for the redline. Attached is a consolidated position from Redwood (RWI_Redline_consolidated.docx) that incorporates Laura's proposed language and a couple of commercial clarifications. High level: 

• Telemetry: suggested compromise language to split operational logs vs. analytics for product improvement (see sec.2.1).
• SCCs: we will adopt the EU SCCs and add a limited Transfer Impact Addendum approach — only applies for non-standard hosting requests and requires 30-day notice.
• Evidence: we can provide redacted SOC2 Type II (2027) by 7/04 and a current subprocessor inventory with onboarding dates by 7/03. We request acceptance of a rolling evidence handoff rather than conditioning signatures on physical delivery of all items.

Next steps proposed: 
1) Paxion confirms whether the telemetry compromise is acceptable to your privacy team.
2) If yes, we upload the consolidated redline into DocuSign for signature on the DPA + Order Form. DocuSign link: https://docusign.example.com/tx/abcdef123456 (placeholder).
3) Security will deliver the SOC2 and subprocessor inventory on the dates above.

Attachments: RWI_Redline_consolidated.docx (docx)

If you prefer a short 30-min legal sync instead of redline back-and-forth, happy to set one up tomorrow morning — availability 8:00–10:00 PT.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference


From: Clara Sato <clara.sato@paxionhealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@paxionhealth.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2028-07-02T16:45:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Paxion DPA — consolidated redline and next steps

Thanks Ben — received the consolidated redline and appreciate the clear timeline. A couple quick confirmations from Paxion legal/security: 

• Telemetry language looks workable but we will ask one editorial tweak to ensure \"anonymized product analytics\" is explicitly out of the Controller carve. Will send a one-line edit.
• SOC2: 7/04 redacted delivery is acceptable. Please ensure the redaction does not remove scope statements or the auditor conclusion.
• Subprocessor list: 7/03 delivery works; Paxion expects a CSV with vendor name, reason for processing, hosting region, and onboarding date.

On KMS/encryption — Marcus asked whether Redwood supports customer-managed keys (bring-your-own-key) for order forms that require it. If that's possible, please flag whether it triggers a pricing uplift or procurement lead time.

We can do the 30-minute sync on 7/02 at 09:00 PT if you have someone from security (or Laura) available. Otherwise we'll proceed with the DocuSign once the one-line telemetry tweak is agreed.

Clara Sato
Legal Ops, Paxion Health

title: Onboarding kickoff — architecture & token profile confirmation
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: María González <maria.gonzalez@astrahealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-06-28T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Onboarding kickoff — architecture & token profile confirmation

Hi María — great to meet you on Friday. Per our call, starting this thread to capture the few items engineering will need to size the onboarding: context length needs, expected tokens per request, and peak/day concurrency. Quick asks below so we can lock the model shortlist and produce a capacity estimate by Tuesday.

1) Context length: what is the typical customer prompt + history length you expect (in tokens or words)? Any cases >8k tokens that are common?
2) Average tokens per generated response and median tokens for retrieval-augmented replies.

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: María González <maria.gonzalez@astrahealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-07-01T08:47:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Onboarding kickoff — architecture & token profile confirmation

Great notes María — thanks for the spreadsheet. Based on your numbers, here is a recommended split and first-pass sizing: attach: redwood_model_shortlist.pdf

Shortlist (by workload tier):
- Tier A (PHI flows — summary + triage): private endpoint, model: redwood-flan-xl-q4 (quantized, FP16 fallback). Context: provision for 4k tokens; design for p95 latency < 400ms.
- Tier B (non-PHI RAG answers): hosted/dedicated mix, model: open-llama-13B-qa (quantized) for cost-efficiency; a higher-capacity variant (flan-xxl) for long summaries above 2k tokens.
- Tier C (low-latency short responses): opt into Redwood caching + 2-token prefix caching for common system prompts; model: small distilled variant.

Sizing/back-of-envelope: with median 220 tokens out and 500 tokens in (context + prompt) — ~720 tokens/req. Pilot steady-state 45 reqs/min -> ~32k tokens/min (480k tokens/hour). Peak bursts 220 reqs/min -> ~158k tokens/min.

Infrastructure ask: for PHI flows we recommend Private deployment with a reserve of 2xGPUs for redundancy during the pilot (can autoscale, but reserve ensures predictable latency). For non-PHI, we can place on Dedicated capacity and use model routing rules (cheaper quantized model for non-critical traffic, fallback to larger model for long summaries).

Next steps:
- Monica, can CS/Implementation take the lead on a 1-week engineering kickoff (scope, playbook, test datasets)?
- María, can you confirm which endpoints are PHI vs non-PHI (spreadsheet marked but please confirm)?
- I'll upload the shortlist PDF and a Drive folder with example prompts and token-calculation workbooks.

Ben

title: Re: Invoice spike from bulk-export job — proposed reconciliation
preview:
From: Elena Ortiz <ops@fablehealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 29 Jun 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Unexpected spike in embedding charges from bulk-export job

Ben —

Heads up: we noticed a large unexpected spike on yesterday's invoice tied to several bulk embedding jobs that ran overnight (job IDs listed in the CSV attached to this message we sent to Finance). Total looks roughly $14k higher than our rolling average. Can you confirm whether this is expected or if something in your system retried/duplicated calls? We throttled the job on our side but need a quick read so we can explain to our CFO.

Appreciate a quick confirmation and any immediate mitigation steps. We can provide logs from our side if helpful.

—Elena Ortiz
Head of Ops, Fable Health
ops@fablehealth.com

Attachment: fable_export_job_ids_20280629.csv (CSV list of job IDs and timestamps)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ortiz <ops@fablehealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 29 Jun 2028 09:34:12 -0700
Subject: Re: Unexpected spike in embedding charges from bulk-export job

Elena —

Thanks for flagging this so quickly and for including the job IDs. I’m really sorry for the surprise here. I’ve looped in our billing lead, Laura, and our incident engineer, Neha, to investigate right away.

Immediate next steps from us:
- Neha will review runtime logs and billing metering to see if calls were double-counted or retried at the infra layer.
- Laura will pull a billing snapshot and draft an interim credit estimate if the anomaly tracks to our side.
- I’ll keep you updated in this thread and aim to send an initial assessment within 4 hours.

We know CFO conversations are time-sensitive — I’ll prioritize this.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Lead, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0123

From: Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Elena Ortiz <ops@fablehealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 29 Jun 2028 11:03:45 -0700
Subject: Re: Unexpected spike in embedding charges from bulk-export job

Hi Elena / Ben —

Short technical summary from our end after an initial log sweep:
- Between 2028-06-28 23:40:00 UTC and 2028-06-29 02:05:00 UTC an autoscaling controller bug caused a small subset of GPU workers to restart and replay in-flight requests during windowed snapshotting.
- For certain long-running bulk embedding jobs (your job IDs 8801–8812), the KV-cache replay plus an in-flight retry path resulted in duplicate token invoicing for the overlapped segments. The runtime emitted the same request twice to the metering pipeline before dedup completed.
- We see duplicated token counts only for jobs with sequence-length > 2k where prefix caching was active; short requests are unaffected.

Evidence attached: billing snapshot (CSV) with a 1.12x duplication factor on affected tokens, and a concise postmortem draft. The root cause is an ordering edge in the replay buffer during worker restart; fix is in review and will be deployed to production in the next 24 hours.

Proposed remediation steps:
1) Immediate: mark affected invoices as \"under review\" and create a provisional credit equal to the duplicated portion (we estimate $14,320 based on your exported usage).
2) Follow-up: apply a permanent credit to the July invoice and provide a line-item export showing the adjustment.
3) Preventative: deploy fix (patch committed) + add an end-to-end metering dedup check in the ingestion path.

I’m happy to jump on a quick call to walk through the logs if useful.

Neha Kapoor
Senior Site Reliability Engineer, Redwood Inference
neha@redwood.com

Attachments: redwood_billing_snapshot_2028-06-29.csv; postmortem-summary-2028-06-29.pdf

Technical note (quoted log snippet):
On Fri Jun 29 2028 00:12:18 UTC worker-17: replayed request id=ux8-9f (seq_len=2416) — metering emitted token_count=2416
On Fri Jun 29 2028 00:12:19 UTC worker-21: duplicate emission for request id=ux8-9f — metering emitted token_count=2416

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ortiz <ops@fablehealth.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 29 Jun 2028 13:18:02 -0700
Subject: Re: Invoice spike from bulk-export job — proposed reconciliation

Elena —

Thank you for your patience and for providing the job IDs. Based on Neha’s analysis and our billing snapshot, here is a concrete proposal:

- We will issue a credit of $14,320 (the duplicated-charge portion) to Fable Health as a one-time adjustment.
- We will mark the disputed portion of the June invoice as \"under review\" so your accounting team is not charged while we finalize paperwork.
- We will provide a downloadable adjustment file and a short audit log showing the duplicated emissions for your records.

Next steps for approval:
1) If Fable confirms acceptance, we will generate an adjustment notice and route it for electronic signature (DocuSign link below).
2) Upon signature we will post the credit to your account and issue an adjusted invoice PDF.

DocuSign link (proposal): https://na1.docusign.net/adjustment/FH-20280629
Postmortem (internal/external-readable): https://drive.google.com/open?id=1a2b3c-postmortem-fable-2028

Please reply with a confirm or any additional questions and I’ll get the DocuSign started.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Account Lead, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com

Attachment: adjustment-estimate-Fable-2028-06-29.pdf (one-page calculation)

From: Elena Ortiz <ops@fablehealth.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Cc: Fable Health Finance <finance@fablehealth.com>
Date: Sat, 30 Jun 2028 15:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Invoice spike from bulk-export job — proposed reconciliation

Ben / Laura —

Thanks for the fast turnaround and the clear remediation steps. Fable accepts the $14,320 credit and we’ll sign the adjustment notice. A couple of asks before we close out:
- Could you include a concise CSV that maps our job IDs to the duplicated token counts (so our finance team can reconcile line-items)?
- Please include a short stamped audit log (UTC timestamps) for the duplicated emissions for compliance.

We’ll route the DocuSign today and confirm when signed. Appreciate the swift remediation and the postmortem link — we’ll review the root cause details.

Best,
Elena Ortiz
Head of Ops, Fable Health

Attachment: fable_finance_request_20280630.pdf (one-paragraph reconciliation request)

From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
To: Elena Ortiz <ops@fablehealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 30 Jun 2028 15:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Invoice spike from bulk-export job — proposed reconciliation (final)

Elena —

Following up from Ben: we’ve prepared the adjustment notice and the CSV map you requested; I’ve attached an interim PDF that summarizes the credit and how it will appear on your account. Once the DocuSign completes we’ll post the credit and send the final adjusted invoice and the stamped audit log (CSV).

title: Request: Temporary SSO for multiple auditors + timezone window
preview:
From: Pamela York <pamela@acme-corp.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: audit-coordinator@acme-corp.com
Date: 2028-06-28T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: SOC 2 Type II access + two auditors (timezone constraints)

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the quick onboarding call. Acme's procurement team needs to download the SOC 2 Type II report and get a short bridge letter for an interim coverage check. Two items that affect how we provision access:

- We have two external audit firms that will need read-only access to the trust portal (one is PW Audit, another is a US-based subcontractor). They will not be in the same organization.
- The subcontractor can only operate between 10:00-18:00 GMT due to timezone constraints and local rules.

Can Redwood provision separate one-time SSO invites for each auditor, set a one-week ephemeral download window, and optionally lock downloads to the auditor IP ranges we provide? Also — if we need a bridge letter that references an audit-period gap (April 1st–May 31st), can you point me to the right contact and a draft?

Attachments: auditor-invite-list.csv (names + emails).

Best,
Pamela York
Vendor Contracts, Acme Corp


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>
To: Pamela York <pamela@acme-corp.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2028-06-28T11:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: SOC 2 Type II access + two auditors (timezone constraints)

Hi Pamela —

Thanks for the clear asks. We can do separate, timeboxed SSO invites and an IP allowlist for the subcontractor. Quick clarifications so we provision correctly:

1) Desired window: you suggested one week. Do you want the window to be contiguous (7 days straight) or two separate 3/4-day windows to align with each auditor?
2) IP ranges: please paste the CIDR(s) in reply or upload to the same CSV and we'll add them to the trust portal rule.
3) Bridge letter: we can provide a short bridge letter referencing the SOC 2 Type II coverage and any gap dates — Marissa (security/compliance) will draft and shared as a Word doc for redlines.

I will loop in Marissa to draft the bridge letter and Karthik to schedule the ephemeral invites with the right TTL and download controls. We will include an audit log export for your records.

Proposed next steps:
- Pamela: confirm contiguous 7-day window or split windows.
- Pamela: paste CIDRs or confirm that auditor emails in CSV include the IP ranges.
- Redwood: Marissa will circulate a bridge-letter draft the same day and Karthik will create one-time SSO links.

Thanks —
Ava Chen
Partnerships, Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2028-06-28T13:20:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: SOC 2 Type II access + two auditors (timezone constraints)

Ava / Karthik — forwarding Pamela's request. Draft notes below so we have a single plan: 

- Provide two separate SSO one-time links tied to auditor emails; TTL 7 days starting when each auditor first clicks the link. Link expiry should be visible in the portal and in the invitation email.
- For the subcontractor limited to 10:00-18:00 GMT: create an IP allowlist rule covering submitted CIDRs; additionally, add a portal session policy that blocks downloads outside that range. (Ben: can you confirm the session restriction is possible for a third-party click?)
- Prepare a bridge letter template that references latest SOC 2 Type II report (redacted pdf attached) and includes the audit-period clarification paragraph for April 1–May 31, 2028.
- Evidence download: enable view-only by default, but allow PDF bundle download when auditor authenticates from an allowlisted IP. Log all activity; produce audit log CSV.

Ben: can you provision the two invites in the trust portal and attach the SSO token strings to the HubSpot deal? I'll draft the bridge letter and share for legal review.

Attachment: SOC2-TypeII-2027-redacted.pdf (redacted for distribution).

-Marissa
Security & Compliance, Redwood Inference


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2028-06-29T08:02:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Fwd: SOC 2 Type II access + two auditors (timezone constraints)

Marissa / team — implementation note:

- Portal supports per-invite TTL and per-invite IP restrictions. I will create two invites:

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>

Subject: Sandbox onboarding: SDK quickstart, auth & embeddings follow-up

Hi Allison / Samir — thanks again for the call earlier. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we have alignment before the technical deep-dive: 

Summary of what we covered:
- BrightPath goal: add semantic search + a reranker layer to improve FAQ and support search relevance (expect ~50 rps peak per instance).
- Integration surface: SDK-based client (Python + TypeScript) that will call Redwood for embeddings, generation (structured output/tool-calling), and a lightweight reranker.
- Security: BrightPath wants to validate auth patterns (API keys, short-lived tokens), audit logs, and isolation for PII-sensitive requests.

Action items I committed to: 
1) Share an SDK quickstart bundle (Python + TS sample apps, docker compose for local dev).
2) Provide a step-by-step quickstart for auth, routing, and structured output (tool calling).
3) Recommend an embeddings+rerank flow and minimal storage pattern.
4) Schedule a 60-minute technical runbook with your engineering team — propose Tue/Wed next week, mornings PT.

What I need from you: 
- Confirm who will join the runbook (engineer + security/infra).
- Attach or confirm the security questionnaire sections you want us to fill (we can prefill parts).

Deliverables attached: redwood-sdk-quickstart.zip (sample apps + README). I also attached a link to the SDK docs in the body.

Next steps: I’ll loop in Karthik (runtime/integration) to scope local dev steps and sample code. In the meantime, please confirm one of the following slots for the runbook: Tue Jul 3 9:00–10:00 PT or Wed Jul 4 10:00–11:00 PT.

Thanks — looking forward.

—
Ava Chen
Developer Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference

- For the QA assistant we want structured JSON returned (intent, action, arguments). Is that done via the generation endpoint with a schema param, or do we need the function-calling abstraction?

4) Embeddings & reranker
- High level: compute embeddings server-side and store in existing Pinecone instance. For reranking, do you recommend a separate fine-tuned cross-encoder on our side or using Redwood's reranker endpoint? Any latency/cost tradeoffs to call out?

5) Local dev
- Our engineers prefer Docker-based local dev, TypeScript samples. Please make sure the quickstart supports npm install and a docker-compose-based sandbox.

>

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
To: Allison Park <allison.park@brightpath.ai>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 29 Jun 2028 16:00:12 -0700
Subject: Re: Sandbox onboarding: SDK quickstart, auth & embeddings follow-up

Hi Allison — quick technical notes to answer the questions and give the team runnable steps for local testing. I’ve included inline examples for Python and TypeScript. The quickstart zip Ava attached contains the same examples and a docker-compose to run a local dev proxy.

1) Auth patterns
- Supported: Long-lived API keys (for non-sensitive quickstarts) and short-lived tokens (recommended for production). Short-lived tokens are minted via our control-plane SSO/KMS integration (we also support OIDC broker).
- Recommendation: Use short-lived tokens for server-side services that sign requests on behalf of users. Store provisioning secrets in your KMS and mint tokens with a 1–2 hour TTL.

Example: environment variable approach (local dev)
- REDWOOD_API_KEY=sk_live_xxx (quickstart only)
- For short-lived flow, a service issues a temporary token that your app uses as Authorization: Bearer <token>.

2) Region routing
- SDK supports per-request region override. By default the client is initialized with a region (e.g., us-west-2) but any request call accepts an optional region parameter for cross-region failover. We also expose a routing policy: primaryRegion + failoverRegions list.
- Quickstart: set REDWOOD_REGION env var or pass region='us-west-2' to the client.

3) Structured output / tool calling
- Two options: (A) function/tool calling abstraction (recommended if you have defined actions), or (B) strict schema validation on the generation endpoint using the schema param.
- If you want typed JSON responses with guaranteed shape, use the function calling mode and define your tools with argument schemas. The SDK will return a structured call result you can map to your handler.

Python (generation + function calling minimal example)
pip install redwood-sdk

from redwood import RedwoodClient
client = RedwoodClient(api_key=os.environ["REDWOOD_API_KEY"], region=os.environ.get("REDWOOD_REGION", "us-west-2"))

# declare a tool handler on your side; for call simulation we pass tools to the call
resp = client.generate(
  model="redwood-chat-1",
  input="Find intent and return JSON: user asked to file a bug.",
  tools=[{"name":"create_ticket","args_schema":{"title":"string","priority":"string","body":"string"}}],
  max_tokens=256
)
# resp.tools[0].arguments -> parsed JSON arguments

TypeScript minimal (function calling)
npm install @redwood/inference

import { Redwood } from '@redwood/inference'
const client = new Redwood({ apiKey: process.env.REDWOOD_API_KEY, region: process.env.REDWOOD_REGION || 'us-west-2' })
const resp = await client.generate({ model: 'redwood-chat-1', input: 'Extract intent and arguments for ordering', tools: [{ name: 'order_item', args_schema: { id: 'string', qty: 'number' } }] })
// resp.tools[0].arguments -> structured object

4) Embeddings + reranker patterns
- Pattern A (recommended for latency-sensitive): Precompute embeddings, store in Pinecone/Vector DB, use ANN to retrieve top-K candidates, then use a light on-the-fly reranker (cross-encoder) via Redwood rerank endpoint to rescore top-N. This keeps recall high while limiting reranker cost.
- Pattern B (lower infra on your side): Use Redwood's hosted reranker endpoint (we offer a rerank API that accepts (query, candidates) and returns scores). Latency & cost: calling hosted reranker for N=20 is ~2x the cost of a single generation request but typically faster than running a heavyweight cross-encoder on-prem.

Example flow: 
- compute embeddings -> store in Pinecone
- query top-50 -> call Redwood rerank for top-10 -> final selection

5) Local dev & docker-compose
- quickstart includes docker-compose.yml with:
  - local-proxy (exposes /v1 -> Redwood sandbox via token)
  - python-sandbox app (runs sample endpoint)
  - ts-sample app

Run locally: 
- unzip redwood-sdk-quickstart.zip
- cp .env.example .env && set REDWOOD_API_KEY and REDWOOD_REGION
- docker-compose up --build

6) Extra notes on cost/perf and quantization
- For local testing use smaller footprints (gpt-small-like) in the sandbox. For production pick a production profile from our model catalog and let Redwood Optimize suggest batching/quantization changes.

Attachments included in quickstart: 
- python-app/ (sample server, req/resp examples)
- ts-app/ (TypeScript sample with Express + Jest smoke tests)
- docker-compose.yml (local proxy + apps)
- README.md (runbook + troubleshooting)

Links:
- SDK quickstart docs: https://redwood.ai/docs/sdk/quickstart
- Reranker API doc: https://redwood.ai/docs/api/rerank

If you want, I can prep a 30–45 minute subset runbook that walks through: environment setup, running python-app smoke tests, wiring to Pinecone, and an end-to-end retrieval + rerank demo. Ava — I’ll be on the call for the tech runbook.

—
Karthik Iyer
Senior Integration Engineer | Redwood Inference
karthik.iyer@redwood.ai

Attachments: redwood-sdk-quickstart.zip (application/zip, 2.1 MB)

To: Allison Park <allison.park@brightpath.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 29 Jun 2028 16:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Sandbox onboarding: SDK quickstart, auth & embeddings follow-up

Great — thanks for the fast reply and for confirming Tue Jul 3 9:00 PT.

Next steps I’ll take now:

title: Post-demo: recommended model pack, JSON schema checks, and next steps
preview:
From: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
To: Sara Lopez <sara@elmbridgehealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-06-25T15:10:00-07:00
Subject: Post-demo: recommended model pack, JSON schema checks, and next steps

Sara / Tom — thanks again for taking the demo today. Quick recap and a concrete plan so we keep momentum:

Summary of today's alignment
- Primary goal: extract structured clinical summaries from intake notes to populate Elmbridge's care management workflow.
- Success criteria: >= 92% slot-level precision on core fields (diagnosis, suggested care plan, urgency) plus JSON-schema compliance for downstream ingestion.
- Latency SLO: p95 <= 350ms for single-request flows in the US region.

Recommended next steps (proposed cadence)
1) Run the evaluation bundle we discussed (benchmark spreadsheet + prompt set) against 3 candidate model variants. Ben will attach the recommended configs. (Target: 1 week)
2) Provide a small sample of 200 anonymized notes for an initial out-of-box pass — we can run structured output tests and return regression metrics.
3) Joint schema acceptance test: Redwood will provide schema_examples.json and a test harness that validates array/object shapes + retry semantics (exponential backoff for 429/503).
4) Quick follow-up: 30-minute sync next Tue to review initial metrics and pick the model to pilot.

Attachments included: elmbridge_prompt_set_v1.txt, model_benchmarks_q2.xlsx, schema_examples.json, sow-draft-rdi-2394.pdf

Ask: Can you confirm (a) permission to run the 200-record sample and (b) availability for Tue 2028-07-02 10:00am PT?

Open items I flagged for internal follow-up: containerizing schema checks for on-prem customers and clarifying retry timeout defaults (Ben).

Thanks,
Monica
Monica Patel | Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference
monica@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0136

----

Attachment stubs:
- elmbridge_prompt_set_v1.txt (plain text) — curated prompt templates and negative examples
- model_benchmarks_q2.xlsx (xlsx) — candidate model metrics and hardware profiles
- schema_examples.json (json) — example payloads and edge-case tests
- sow-draft-rdi-2394.pdf (pdf) — initial SOW and milestone estimates


From: Tom Nguyen <tom@elmbridgehealth.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Cc: Sara Lopez <sara@elmbridgehealth.com>
Date: 2028-06-25T18:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo: recommended model pack, JSON schema checks, and next steps

Thanks Monica — good recap. A few clarifying questions from our side before we send the sample:

1) Schema validation: when you say \"schema_examples.json\", will the harness validate both shape and semantic constraints (e.g., severity must be one of the allowed enums)? We need a fail/partial-fail mode that marks records as \"needs review\" rather than dropping them.
2) Retries: what's your default retry policy for 429s in production? We have bursty ingestion between 10–11am PT and don't want duplicate writes if the client retries.
3) Candidate models: can you confirm the recommended smaller-CPU footprint variant? We may run a pilot on Dedicated with limited GPUs first.

We can approve the 200-record pass once you confirm the above. Also Tue 7/2 10am PT works for Sara and me.

Tom
Tom Nguyen | Director of Platform Engineering, Elmbridge Health
tn@elmbridgehealth.com | +1 (206) 555-0199

On 2028-06-25T15:10:00-07:00 Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com> wrote:
> Sara / Tom — thanks again for taking the demo today. Quick recap and a concrete plan so we keep momentum:...


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Tom Nguyen <tom@elmbridgehealth.com>, Sara Lopez <sara@elmbridgehealth.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-06-26T08:16:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo: recommended model pack, JSON schema checks, and next steps

Tom — quick answers and what we'll run in the evaluation bundle. Short version up front: yes to enum validation + partial-fail mode, default retry is idempotent client retry with de-dupe token, and recommended pilot model is the \"redwood-7b-quant-v2\" variant for cost/latency balance. Details below.

1) Schema validation behavior- The harness performs two stages: a) syntactic validation against the JSON Schema (required fields, enums, types), b) soft semantic checks (confidence thresholds and field-level heuristics).
- Records failing syntactic validation are returned with an error object; records failing semantic checks get marked with \"review_required\": true. We'll provide a CSV summary and JSONL output so your ingestion logic can mark but not drop these records.

2) Retry policy and duplicate suppression- Default SLO-focused retry: client can retry on 429/503 with exponential backoff (initial 250ms, max 4s, 5 attempts).
- To prevent duplicates we surface a request_id header and recommend the client persist dedupe tokens for 24h. On Dedicated we can extend token TTL.

3) Model recommendations- redwood-7b-quant-v2 (recommended): balanced latency (~180–320ms p95 on g4dn-like hardware), strong structured output extraction.
- redwood-13b-ptq (higher quality): better slot precision (+3–4%), higher p95 latency (~420–550ms). Good for Dedicated pilot.
- redwood-7b-int8 (low-cost): faster and cheaper but may need prompt engineering for edge cases.

4) What we'll run this week (deliverables)- Full run of model_benchmarks_q2.xlsx across the three variants using your sample (sanitized). Metrics: slot precision/recall, schema compliance rate, hallucination flag rate, latency distribution.
- Deliver a results sheet, top 10 failure traces, and a JSONL export with schema pass/fail + review_required flags.
- Sample code to validate schema_examples.json and a lightweight retry helper (node/python snippet).

Attachments (stubs): schema_examples.json, model_benchmarks_q2.xlsx, sample_validation_snippets.zip

If that addresses your questions, Monica / I will get the evaluation-run scheduled as soon as we have the 200-record batch.

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter | Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 (650) 555-0110

----

Example snippet (pseudo):
POST /v1/generate
Headers: {\"Request-Id\": \"<uuid>\", \"Authorization\": \"Bearer <key>\"}
Body: {\"prompt\": \"<structured prompt>\", \"schema\": <reference to schema_examples.json>}

Client retry helper (recommended): initial=250ms, factor=2, max=4s, attempts=5; include request-id for de-dupe.


From: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.com>
To: Tom Nguyen <tom@elmbridgehealth.com>
Cc: Sara Lopez <sara@elmbridgehealth.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Markus Klein <markus@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-06-26T10:04:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Post-demo: recommended model pack, JSON schema checks, and next steps

Perfect — thanks Tom and Ben. Confirming:
- You have permission to run the 200-record sample (Tom) — noted.
- Evaluation run will include the three model variants Ben outlined, with outputs: results sheet, JSONL export, and failure traces.
- We'll include partial-fail semantics in the deliverable and the retry helper snippet.

I've booked Tue 7/2 10:00am PT for the review. I'll share a calendar invite and a recording link for the run.

One ask for the pilot: please annotate 20 of the 200 records with \"expected output\" examples if possible — that helps us triage regressions faster. If Elmbridge can do that, we'll prioritize those records in the report.

Thanks — looking forward to results.
Monica
Monica Patel | Enterprise AE, Redwood Inference

title: Coordinating Day 0: orientation access, cross-team coverage, and joins
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.com>
To: Samir Khan <samir.khan@averyhealth.com>, Priya Nair <priya.nair@averyhealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 25 Jun 2028 15:02:00 -0700
Subject: Coordinating Day 0: orientation access, cross-team coverage, and joins
