title: Allocation appeal: Sprintline surge impacting Reserved GPU tranche
preview:
From: Sara Lim <sara@sprintline.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
Cc: Nathan Ortega <nathan@sprintline.ai>
Date: 2028-05-31T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Unexpected performance / surge on reserved pool - immediate help?

Hi Ava,

We noticed a sudden degradation in throughput on our reserved capacity this morning (p50 latency jumped 3.2x and tail latencies spiked). Our ci jobs and customer-facing inferencing started timing out. Can you confirm whether there was any reallocation or preemption in our tranche? Attached is a quick metric snapshot (sprintline_maup_impact_table.xlsx).

Quick background: we contracted a dedicated tranche for Q2 (committed 8x A100-equivalent hours) and we rely on the reservation for peak traffic bursts. If there's been an encroachment by another deal, we need a remediation plan and timeline.

Ask: can you confirm whether any active burst requests were granted to other accounts overlapping our windows? If so, what are the options — temporary burst swap, compensation, or immediate resegmentation?

Thanks,
Sara Lim
Head of Platform
Sprintline.ai

[Attachment: sprintline_maup_impact_table.xlsx]

From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
To: Sara Lim <sara@sprintline.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-05-31T10:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Unexpected performance / surge on reserved pool - immediate help?

Hi Sara — thanks for flagging and for the snapshot. I'm escalating internally right now.

Karthik / Ben / Monica — Sprintline is seeing a sharp latency regression on their reserved tranche during their 08:30–09:30 daily window. They suspect cross-tenant burst activity. Can ops confirm whether any preemption or manual reallocation occurred between 08:00–10:00 PT? Also attaching their contract term sheet for context.

Sara — we'll get back with a short-term mitigation plan within the hour and a proposed remediation path for the week.

— Ava
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ava@redwood.ai

[Attachment: reserved-tranche-term-sheet.pdf]

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-05-31T10:28:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Unexpected performance / surge on reserved pool - immediate help?

Quick check from infra: no manual preempts logged for Sprintline's tranche between 08:00–10:00 PT. However, an automated burst policy kicked in at 08:12 to accommodate a large short-lived spike from a high-tier customer (RivalCloud) who requested emergency burst capacity while migrating a model. The policy allowed ephemeral capacity borrow from low-utilization reserved tranches when cluster headroom < 7%.

Impact: the borrow window lasted ~14 minutes and overlapped partial prefix cache warmup for Sprintline which pushed their tail latencies up. We have logs and the scheduler decision trace.

Options (short-term):
- Roll back the ephemeral borrow (if still active) — Karthik to confirm;
- Provision temporary burst to Sprintline from unallocated pool I can spin up (+~2 A100 hours) — ETA ~35 minutes;
- Apply a soft-preempt hold for Sprintline hours (configure node-level affinity until 09:30) — requires SRE approval for manual routing override.

I'll stand by to execute whichever you prefer.

-Karthik
SVP, Dedicated Capacity Operations

> On May 31, 2028, Ava Chen wrote:
> > Sara — thanks for flagging and for the snapshot. I'm escalating internally right now.


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-05-31T10:42:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Unexpected performance / surge on reserved pool - immediate help?

Ava / Karthik — quick commercial note: Sprintline is a mid-enterprise renewal scheduled in Q3 with an expansion motion (they're trialing embeddings at high QPS). We should avoid any perception of broken reservation enforcement. If RivalCloud's burst was an approved emergency, we need to track remediation and decide on compensation. Options: one-time credit, temporary bump to their reserved baseline for 2 weeks, or priority scheduling for their next three days of peak windows.

If we take manual routing override, get Marissa looped for approval of any SLA exception / credit.

-ben
Principal Sales Ops

> On May 31, 2028, Karthik Iyer wrote:
> > Quick check from infra: no manual preempts logged for Sprintline's tranche between 08:00–10:00 PT. However, an automated burst policy kicked in at 08:12 ...

From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>
To: Sara Lim <sara@sprintline.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-05-31T11:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Unexpected performance / surge on reserved pool - immediate help?

Sara — short update and immediate remediation steps: 

1) Karthik confirmed the automated burst policy briefly allowed a borrow for RivalCloud at 08:12 which overlapped your warmup window. There was no manual reallocation.
2) Karthik is initiating an immediate temporary +2 A100-hour provision from spare capacity (ETA ~30m).
3) We'll apply a soft-preempt hold and node affinity across your 08:30–09:30 window for the rest of the week to avoid recurrence.
4) Ben will propose a goodwill remediation (credit or short-term baseline bump).

We expect visible improvement once the extra capacity is online; I'll follow up after the 09:00 check-in. We're also opening a post-incident note that will include scheduler trace and recommended policy guardrail changes to prevent similar collisions.

Apologies for the disruption — we take reservations seriously and will make this right.

— Ava

> On May 31, 2028, Ben Carter wrote:
> > If we take manual routing override, get Marissa looped for approval of any SLA exception / credit.


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Sara Lim <sara@sprintline.ai>
Date: 2028-06-04T16:22:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Allocation appeal: Sprintline surge impacting Reserved GPU tranche

Thanks team — appreciate the investigation and the remediation steps. I reviewed the scheduler trace and the automated policy parameters. Two requests from my side: 

1) Short-term: Approve the +2 A100-hour bump and the soft-preempt node affinity for Sprintline for the next 5 business days. Ben, please prepare a one-off credit packet equivalent to 8 hours at their committed rate as a gesture for the impact window.

2) Medium-term: Karthik, let's schedule a policy change to the automated-burst rule: require an emergency override flag and a 15-minute cooldown per cluster when headroom < 5%. Draft the policy and a rollout plan (canary on non-prod clusters first). I want the rollout plan on my calendar by EOW.

Ava, please send Sprintline a concise summary note (what happened, immediate fix, remediation credit, next steps) and loop in their renewal AE once they confirm recovery.

I'll approve the SLA exception for this instance.

Marissa Cole
VP, Customer Success Operations
Redwood Inference

Impact: the borrow window lasted ~14 minutes and overlapped partial prefix cache warmup for Sprintline which pushed their tail latencies up. We have logs and the scheduler decision trace.

Options (short-term):
- Roll back the ephemeral borrow (if still active) — Karthik to confirm;
- Provision temporary burst to Sprintline from unallocated pool I can spin up (+~2 A100 hours) — ETA ~35 minutes;
- Apply a soft-preempt hold for Sprintline hours (configure node-level affinity until 09:30) — requires SRE approval for manual routing override.

I'll stand by to execute whichever you prefer.

-Karthik
SVP, Dedicated Capacity Operations

> On May 31, 2028, Ava Chen wrote:
> > Sara — thanks for flagging and for the snapshot. I'm escalating internally right now.

Date: 2028-05-31T10:42:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Unexpected performance / surge on reserved pool - immediate help?

Ava / Karthik — quick commercial note: Sprintline is a mid-enterprise renewal scheduled in Q3 with an expansion motion (they're trialing embeddings at high QPS). We should avoid any perception of broken reservation enforcement. If RivalCloud's burst was an approved emergency, we need to track remediation and decide on compensation. Options: one-time credit, temporary bump to their reserved baseline for 2 weeks, or priority scheduling for their next three days of peak windows.

If we take manual routing override, get Marissa looped for approval of any SLA exception / credit.

-ben
Principal Sales Ops

> > Quick check from infra: no manual preempts logged for Sprintline's tranche between 08:00–10:00 PT. However, an automated burst policy kicked in at 08:12 ...

Date: 2028-05-31T11:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Unexpected performance / surge on reserved pool - immediate help?

Sara — short update and immediate remediation steps: 

1) Karthik confirmed the automated burst policy briefly allowed a borrow for RivalCloud at 08:12 which overlapped your warmup window. There was no manual reallocation.
2) Karthik is initiating an immediate temporary +2 A100-hour provision from spare capacity (ETA ~30m).
3) We'll apply a soft-preempt hold and node affinity across your 08:30–09:30 window for the rest of the week to avoid recurrence.
4) Ben will propose a goodwill remediation (credit or short-term baseline bump).

We expect visible improvement once the extra capacity is online; I'll follow up after the 09:00 check-in. We're also opening a post-incident note that will include scheduler trace and recommended policy guardrail changes to prevent similar collisions.

Apologies for the disruption — we take reservations seriously and will make this right.

— Ava

Subject: RE: Allocation appeal: Sprintline surge impacting Reserved GPU tranche

Thanks team — appreciate the investigation and the remediation steps. I reviewed the scheduler trace and the automated policy parameters. Two requests from my side: 

1) Short-term: Approve the +2 A100-hour bump and the soft-preempt node affinity for Sprintline for the next 5 business days. Ben, please prepare a one-off credit packet equivalent to 8 hours at their committed rate as a gesture for the impact window.

2) Medium-term: Karthik, let's schedule a policy change to the automated-burst rule: require an emergency override flag and a 15-minute cooldown per cluster when headroom < 5%. Draft the policy and a rollout plan (canary on non-prod clusters first). I want the rollout plan on my calendar by EOW.

title: Intermittent 5xxs & tail latency after peering maintenance — urgent customer escalation

From: Alicia Ramos <alicia.ramos@clearwater-retail.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: ops@clearwater-retail.com
Date: 2028-06-01T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Spike in 5xxs + p99 after peering maintenance (urgent)

Hi Ben —

We started seeing a spike in 5xx errors and ~4–6x increase in p99 latency on calls to your inference endpoints starting around 22:30 UTC last night. This is impacting checkout scoring and cart recommendations (customers see long waits or failures). Sample observations attached (clearwater-500-samples.log, p99-traces-20280601.png).

Symptoms:
- Majority are 502/504 with occasional 500s.
- Tail latency jumps to ~1.2s on text-generation requests (baseline ~80ms).
- Errors correlate with the times of calls that route through the new peering path announced in your status page maintenance.

We haven't changed anything on our side. Can you triage and confirm whether this is on Redwood's peering/firewall/proxy side? We need an ETA — this is blocking a high-volume A/B test and at risk of conversion impact.

Thanks,
Alicia Ramos
Director, Platform Reliability
Clearwater Retail
[Attachment: clearwater-500-samples.log (text)]
[Attachment: p99-traces-20280601.png (image)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-06-01T09:29:00-07:00
Subject: FW: Spike in 5xxs + p99 after peering maintenance — Clearwater (needs triage, exec brief)

Team —

Customer: Clearwater Retail (CLR-4321). They report pervasive 502/504s and p99 jumps beginning 22:30 UTC after our peering maintenance window. They are high-priority (large conversion impact).

Please triage in this order:
1) Confirm whether peering path is announcing the correct route and whether any ACLs/firewall rules changed during maintenance.
2) Check proxy/nginx/gateway logs for increased upstream resets or 502 generation reasons.
3) If this is a network-level policy mismatch, propose immediate rollback plan (low risk) and a longer-term mitigation.

Marissa — please prepare a one-slide exec note for the CTO/CSO if we need to loop them. Soojin/Vivek please coordinate on customer comms.

Ben

From: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-06-01T09:44:00-07:00
Subject: RE: FW: Spike in 5xxs + p99 after peering maintenance — initial network triage

Quick summary of what I've found so far (reading syslogs + partner route table):
- At 22:27 UTC last night our transit partner applied a peering maintenance change that included an updated firewall policy on the transit hop between us and the Clearwater region.
- Packet counters on the transit firewall show a sharp increase in TCP RSTs and a small but meaningful number of dropped SYNs toward our inference cluster subnets.
- Proxy/nginx error logs show upstream connection resets concentrated on backend nodes served via the new path (matches customer's affected calls).
- The policy change includes a new stateful inspection rule that appears to classify some large POSTs (long body inference requests) as suspicious (false-positive) and triggers resets.

Proposed immediate action (30–45m window):
1) Ask transit partner to rollback the specific firewall policy (targeted rollback only).
2) If rollback not possible, advertise a temporary BGP prepended alternate path to avoid the transit hop for Clearwater routes.
3) Enable a short-lived, route-based rate-limit relaxation at our gateway to reduce false-positive matches while partner fixes their ACLs.

Risks: targeted rollback is low-risk; BGP prepending will re-route traffic and may affect latency for a small subset but should restore availability quickly.

I'll open a Jira and include packet captures and ACL diff (network-acl-diff.txt).

—Soojin
Soojin Lee
Network Engineer, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: network-acl-diff.txt (text)]

From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>

Ben / team —

Marissa — attaching the short exec note for the CTO (one slide). If we need a formal exec escalation to the CSO, I recommend we include the potential revenue impact estimate (I'll coordinate with finance).

I'll ping the partner contacts now.

—Vivek
Customer Ops, Redwood Inference

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alicia Ramos <alicia.ramos@clearwater-retail.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-06-01T10:18:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Spike in 5xxs + p99 after peering maintenance (status update)

Alicia —

Quick update: our initial triage shows a transit-side firewall policy change during peering maintenance that is causing resets for some inference request flows. We're working with the transit partner now. Plan A: partner rollback of the ACL (requested). Plan B (if rollback delayed): temporary BGP prepend + gateway rate-limit relaxation from our side. We expect to have a containment action started within 30–45 minutes.

I've opened INFRA-2468 and will paste live updates there: https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/INFRA-2468

Thanks for your patience — we know this is impacting key flows.

—Ben

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alicia Ramos <alicia.ramos@clearwater-retail.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.com>
Date: 2028-06-02T16:45:00-07:00
Subject: RESOLVED: Root cause and follow-up (Clearwater outage containment)

Alicia —

Follow-up and resolution summary:
Root cause: a misapplied transit firewall policy during the partner's peering maintenance incorrectly matched long inference POST requests and triggered stateful resets. That caused connection resets and elevated 5xxs and p99 latency for traffic transiting that hop.

What we did:
- Partner rolled back the offending ACL at 23:05 UTC (same-day), restoring normal flow for most requests.
- We applied a temporary BGP prepend for the Clearwater prefixes while we validated the full recovery (rolled back once partners confirmed clean).
- Our gateway rate-limit relaxation was applied for 1 hour to reduce false positives while partner validated their policy.

Customer impact: transient 502/504s and p99 spikes between 22:30–23:40 UTC; services restored to baseline after rollback.

Action items / next steps:
1) Postmortem to include checklist for peering change coordination and an automated ACL diff check on partner changes (owner: Soojin, due 2028-06-09).
2) Update status page automation to flag route-level firewall policy changes that our monitoring can ingest (owner: Marissa, due 2028-06-12).
3) Compensation/credits conversation — Ben to work with Clearwater on commercial remediation if required.

I'll keep this thread open for any follow-ups. Thank you for working with us through the incident — truly appreciate the patience.

Warmly,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: customer-impact-summary.pdf (pdf)]

title: Hosted API commit top-up + rateband and latency SLA request
preview:
From: Alicia Moreno <alicia@cartly.io>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-05-28T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Hosted API commit top-up & rateband adjustment

Hi Aditya / Ben,

Quick note from Cartly — over the last 6 weeks we've grown a lot faster than forecast and are hitting throttles and increased p95 latencies on the hosted endpoint (we're seeing spikes during both PST morning and late-EMEA evening windows). We reviewed our May usage (attached) and we'd like to discuss:

- Increasing our monthly commit from 1.2M tokens to 2.5M tokens (starting June billing period).
- Moving up one usage tier so we aren't subject to the current 60 req/min soft rate during peak windows — ideally a 120 req/min sustained cap with short burst allowance to 240 for 10m windows.
- Clarification on latency SLA at the new rateband (p95 target) and whether there's a temporary grace period while we migrate traffic.

We can sign an amendment to move to the higher commitment if we can get predictable rate limits and a short-term latency mitigation plan. Attached: usage_report_may.csv and a trimmed latency log (qos_latency_log.txt).

Can we set up a call this week? We are flexible on Thursday morning PST.

Thanks,
Alicia Moreno
Head of Platform - Cartly Inc.
procureops@cartly.io

Attachments: usage_report_may.csv (csv), qos_latency_log.txt (text)


From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Alicia Moreno <alicia@cartly.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-05-28T11:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Hosted API commit top-up & rateband adjustment

Hi Alicia — thanks for the heads up and for the attachments. Appreciate the detail.

Short answers up front: yes, we can support a higher monthly commit and a higher sustained rate tier; we typically model pricing in three components (base commit, sustained rate tier, burst capacity). We'll need a couple of specifics from your side to map to the right rateband and to give an SLA commitment:

1) Peak distribution: what % of traffic occurs in the two PST / EMEA windows you mentioned?
2) Are the spikes coming from retries or new user sessions?
3) Do you have any planned launch / marketing events in June that could further increase burst risk?

Kimberly, can you review Cartly's latency logs and propose an interim mitigation (routing/batching/priority lane) we can offer during the first 30 days after the amendment goes live?

Alicia — I can take a short call Thursday 08:30-09:30 PT. If that works I’ll send a calendar invite. Assuming the numbers look like the May report, I’ll draft an amendment with a stepped commit (2.5M month 1, 2.8M month 2) and an explicit burst window clause.

— Aditya
Aditya Rao
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.ai



From: Alicia Moreno <alicia@cartly.io>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-05-28T13:20:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Hosted API commit top-up & rateband adjustment

Thanks Aditya — appreciate the quick response.

Answers:
- ~58% of token volume happens in the PST morning window (07:00-10:30 PT), ~22% in the evening EMEA window (17:00-20:00 PT), the rest is distributed.
- Spikes are mostly new sessions as we rolled a small product change last month — retry rates are low (~1.2%).
- No major launches planned in June beyond some organic promotions.

We can share our traffic_profile.yaml if that helps with sizing. On the SLA question: we need a documented p95 latency target that is achievable with the stepped commit — even a 45-day mitigation window would be workable while we smooth traffic.

Let me know when you send the invite.

Best,
Alicia

Attachments: traffic_profile.yaml (yaml)


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.ai>
To: Alicia Moreno <alicia@cartly.io>
Cc: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-05-29T10:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Hosted API commit top-up & rateband adjustment

Hi Alicia — I dug into the logs and your traffic profile. A few technical notes and an interim plan: 

- Root cause: traffic concentration around session start is increasing KV cache misses and fragmentation across worker shards which raises p95.
- Short-term mitigation (30–45 days): enable request affinity (session pinning) + a temporary routing priority for Cartly to the higher-throughput pool during PST morning windows. This reduces cross-shard cache churn.
- Config: sustain 120 req/min with a 10-minute burst to 240 req/min, plus a soft backoff at 300 req/min enforced for 2 minutes. We can instrument a per-customer priority lane that gives you lower queueing latency for the commit period.

I recommend this config and we can include it in the amendment as an operational attachment (we'll list the exact routing rule ids). Note: priority lanes are part of the Dedicated offering by default, but we can provide a hosted priority lane as a short-term add-on while you ramp. Pricing impact below.

Estimated commercials (high level) — ballpark: +$18k/mo on top of existing commit for the higher sustained rate and the priority-lane add-on. Final numbers in the amendment.

If you approve the config I’ll prepare an ops runbook and the language to include in the 30–45 day mitigation clause.

— Kimberly Park
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
kimberly@redwood.ai



From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Alicia Moreno <alicia@cartly.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.ai>
Date: 2028-05-30T11:05:00-07:00
Subject: RE: Hosted API commit top-up & rateband adjustment — proposal and next steps

Alicia — thanks for the details and Kim's review. Proposal summary below (if this looks good I’ll kick off a DocuSign amendment):

- Commit: monthly committed tokens increase from 1.2M -> 2.5M starting 2028-06-01. Step increase: 2.5M month 1, 2.8M month 2, then flat.
- Rateband: move to Sustained-2 tier (supports 120 req/min sustained). Burst: 240 req/min for 10 minutes, emergency cap 300 req/min with soft backoff.
- SLA: target p95 <= 420ms for the Hosted API under the Sustained-2 rateband.
- Mitigation window: 45-day operational grace after amendment goes live. Redwood will enable session affinity + hosted priority lane during PST morning window. We will run joint telemetry for the first 14 days and review results.
- Commercials: incremental $18,000/month on top of existing commit; 12-month minimum term for the new commit. First invoice will be pro-rated for June.

Next steps:
1) Alicia — confirm you accept the configuration and the 45-day mitigation window.
2) We will create an amendment (DocuSign) with the exact service-level language and billing update (I’ll send the link to you and your procurement team).
3) Kim’s team will schedule the routing change and a pre-cutover test window.

If you confirm we’ll aim to have the amendment sent today and the call to schedule the cutover tomorrow morning.

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.ai

Attachment: amendment_draft.pdf (pdf)

title: Banking handoff + PO payload acceptance — Acme Logistics
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Kim <ops@acmelogistics.com>
Cc: contracts@acmelogistics.com
Date: Wed, 25 May 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Vendor onboarding: Banking info + PO mapping

Hi Alex,

Thanks for starting the onboarding process. To get payments flowing for the pilot engagement, can you confirm the following by EOD so Finance can run the verification: 

- Signed bank details for both ACH and wire (routing + account + beneficiary name + bank SWIFT for wires)
- W-9 or VAT registration (depending on entity)
- Preferred remittance format (sample invoice-to-PO mapping)

Attached is our onboarding form to make this quicker — please fill the bank section and return (acme_onboarding_form.pdf). If you prefer, you can upload the completed form to the secure link in the HubSpot deal (I'll paste a link after we confirm).

A couple of notes on PO/Invoice mapping we need to lock down: 
- PO ID will be sent in the invoice metadata field named: rdw_po_id
- Invoice number needs to be in invoice_id
- For remittance we prefer a single CSV string per payment: PO|INVOICE|AMOUNT (example: PO-457|INV-334|3200.00)

Once we have your banking proof we will schedule two micro-deposits (USD) to validate the account — those typically clear in 2–3 business days. After micro-deposit verification, Finance will accept the bank and enable the account for live rails.

Attachments: acme_onboarding_form.pdf (fillable PDF)

Thanks — looping in Finance for visibility.

—
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0172

preview:
From: Lena Ortiz <lena.ortiz@meridianhealth.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Rajiv Menon <rajiv.menon@meridianhealth.com>, procurement@meridianhealth.com
Date: Fri, 25 May 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Entity remit, wiring instructions, and Net-terms for Order Form

Hi Kimberly —

Thanks for the updated Order Form (v3). Before we route to procurement for signature we need three quick confirmations so our AP team can accept the documents and wire payments correctly:

1) Which Redwood legal entity will appear on the invoice and receive payment (US EIN and address)?
2) Can Redwood accept Net45 for this order (pilot + 12-month term)? Our standard is Net45 for strategic vendors.
3) We need a copy of the Redwood tax certificate (or W-9) and wire instructions for ACH/wire.

We have an internal PO ready but procurement requires the remit entity and tax evidence to be present on the Order Form before we pull PO/commit. Also note our tax team asked for confirmation on whether Redwood will treat these invoices as US-sourced (no VAT) or will invoice from an EU entity (VAT applicable).

Attached: current OrderForm_v3 from your team (we redlined a couple of fields). Please respond by EOD PT if possible — procurement is on a tight activation window.

Best,
Lena Ortiz
Sr. Procurement Manager | Meridian Health
Lena Ortiz
[attachment: Meridian_OrderForm_v3_comments.pdf (pdf)]

Hi Lena —

Thanks for the clear ask and for the redlines. Quick answers below and a couple of proposed options so we can keep the PO flow moving:

1) Invoice remit entity: Redwood Inference, Inc. (US)
   - EIN: 12-3456789
   - Address: 401 Redwood Ave, San Francisco, CA 94107
   - We will invoice from our US entity (no VAT).

2) Net terms: Our standard for self-serve customers is Net30, but for enterprise pilots we can accept Net45 for the initial 12-month term if Meridian agrees to a 1% early-payment discount (if paid within 15 days). If Net45 is mandatory without discount, finance has asked for a 30-day reserve (activation hold) until the first invoice clears.

3) Tax evidence & wires: attaching our W-9 / tax certificate and standard wire/ACH instructions.

Next steps I propose:
- We attach Redwood_Tax_Certificate_2028.pdf to the Order Form and populate the Invoice Remit block with the US entity details.
- Ben (cc’d) will prepare a short finance addendum capturing the Net45 vs Net30 fallback and the 1% early-pay discount option for the PO.
- If Meridian confirms remit to US entity and the PO number, we can update the OF and send for signature (DocuSign link included below).

I’ll loop Legal if you need any MSA redline changes for tax or remit language, but we think updating the Order Form and adding the W-9 should be sufficient.

DocuSign (pre-fill): https://www.docusign.net/Member/PowerFormSigning.aspx?env=prod&acct=ABC123&ppid=XYZ789

Thanks — aiming to get this wrapped today.

—Kimberly Park

Subject: Renewal planning: Foundry renew + capacity uplift — mutual action & QBR scheduling

Hi Lena / Aaron —

Thanks for the early heads-up on the renewal. I pulled together a brief mutual action plan and a proposed QBR agenda to get us moving. High level asks from our side: confirm expected throughput uplift (token/month and peak windows), primary procurement/legal contact, and any new security/compliance gating that we should route early.

Proposed timeline (flexible):
- Week of Jun 12: renewal review & procurement kickoff
- Week of Jun 26: executive QBR (options on calendar below)
- Jul 1–15: finalize paperwork and onboarding for uplift

Attachments: 20280525_Foundry_MAP_v1.xlsx (mutual action plan) and foundry_qbr_agenda.pdf (QBR draft).

Can you confirm:
1) Who should we loop for procurement + legal?
2) Is the uplift primarily predictable monthly volume or are there seasonal spikes we should plan for?
3) Preferred QBR week/day (Jun 26–30 or Jun 29–30 are our current suggestions).

I'll loop in our technical and governance leads once we have procurement contact.

Thanks — looking forward to getting this on the calendar.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0198
Redwood Inference

[Attachment: 20280525_Foundry_MAP_v1.xlsx (Excel, 48KB)]
[Attachment: foundry_qbr_agenda.pdf (PDF, 22KB)]

From: Lena Park <lena.park@foundry.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Aaron Mills <aaron.mills@foundry.ai>
Cc: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 25 May 2028 12:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal planning: Foundry renew + capacity uplift — mutual action & QBR scheduling

Thanks Ben — appreciate the concise plan. A few quick items from Foundry: 
- Procurement: procurement@foundry.ai (please cc Marsha Kim in Legal — marsha.kim@foundry.ai)
- Uplift: Aaron will attach the expected delta: currently +35% steady-state tokens/month with a projected spike in Nov–Dec for our holiday campaign (we'll include rough concurrency/peak numbers).
- QBR: Jun 29 works best for our execs (afternoon PT) — is that doable?

Please loop SRE and Customer Success — we'll want a short technical sync to lock in buffering and burst capacity. Aaron will send the forecast CSV later today.

Lena Park

Thanks — quick technical/readout note from Platform/Infra:

1) Capacity: a 35% steady-state uplift is straightforward to cover with a modest pool resize; we can provision a rolling increase to avoid churn on the Dedicated instances. For the Nov spike (3–4x concurrency), we recommend a hybrid approach: a ramped Dedicated uplift (committed) plus a reserve burst policy (buffer burst capacity on shared pools) with automatic routing and a fallback to a smaller quantized model if latency SLOs are at risk.

2) Lead times: for guaranteed Dedicated capacity, we should assume a 2–3 week provisioning runway for committed GPUs. Bursts on hosted pools are quicker but not SLA-backed — we'll document expected latency/cost tradeoffs.

3) Security evidence: Foundry's ask (SOC 2 artifact, retention policy detail, and audit logs) is standard — we have a SOC 2 pack and can export retention config and audit logs for the requested window. I'll coordinate a short security packet and share a secure link to the artifacts.

Proposed next step: a 30-minute tech sync week of Jun 7 to validate concurrency numbers, failover behavior, and QBR demo slice (Marissa, can you coordinate a poll for slots?).

title: Dedicated capacity quick-scan: traffic, concurrency & response-lengths
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@avasolutions.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 25 May 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Dedicated capacity quick-scan: traffic, concurrency & response-lengths

Jordan —

Thanks again for taking time this week. To help our pre-sales sizing team put together a tight dedicated capacity estimate, can you complete the short traffic snapshot below? The goal is to get a first-cut GPU footprint and a few candidate SLO/sku options by end of day Friday.

High-level asks (short):
- Typical and peak requests per minute (TPM / peak TPM)
- Concurrent active sessions (if you use session affinity or long-running context)
- Average prompt length (tokens) and average generation length (tokens) by API use-case (chat vs. single prompt)
- P99 latency target and acceptable p99 tail (e.g., 200ms p99/token or 800ms overall)
- Percent of requests that require streaming vs full-response at once
- Any daily or weekly seasonality (example: 3x peak on Tuesdays 9–11am)

I've attached a tiny CSV template (ava_profile.csv) and a sample payload (example_payload.json) — filling those out is easiest. If you can also share a sampled telemetry snippet (500–1k lines) that shows token distribution and request timestamps, that would be ideal.

Quick note on assumptions our team will use unless you flag otherwise: 8k-context model baseline, mixed-precision quantized runtime, and 4:1 token-to-kernel batching when possible. We'll call out sensitivity to prompt/response lengths in the estimate.

Next step: If you can reply with the CSV and a short note about any spike windows, Ben will draft a candidate GPU mix and Karthik will sanity-check session-affinity assumptions.

Thanks —
Camila Reyes
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
camila.reyes@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0163

Attachments: ava_profile.csv (template), example_payload.json (sample)


From: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@avasolutions.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 25 May 2028 11:27:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity quick-scan: traffic, concurrency & response-lengths

Camila —

Appreciate the template, attached a filled CSV and a short telemetry export. High-level answers inline:

- Typical RPS: 18 req/sec (1,080 rpm), peak RPS: 72 req/sec (4,320 rpm) — peaks are short, 15–30 minutes
- Concurrent sessions: We have a concept of "conversations". At peak we see ~420 active sessions; baseline ~90.
- Prompt lengths: Chat prompts average 150 tokens (user + system), generation average 280 tokens. For async batch jobs prompts are ~600 tokens / response ~1,200 tokens.
- Latency target: p95 < 450ms for chat interactions; willing to accept p99 up to 1.1s for single-request gen.
- Streaming: ~35% streaming for chat (token-by-token), rest are full-response.
- Seasonality: Weekly spike Tuesdays 10:00–10:30 PST, monthly spike on first business day.

Telemetry: attached telemetry_snippet.json (500 lines). Let us know if you need a larger sample.

Jordan
Jordan Lee
Head of ML Engineering | Ava Solutions
jordan.lee@avasolutions.com
+1 (617) 555-0199

Attachments: ava_profile_filled.csv, telemetry_snippet.json


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 25 May 2028 13:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity quick-scan: traffic, concurrency & response-lengths

Team —

Thanks for the numbers. Quick notes / clarifying questions before I build a sketch:
1) Jordan's average vs batch gen numbers are quite different. Do we treat batch jobs as separate queueing workloads (longer tail acceptable) or the same SLO? If separate, we can size two pools: low-latency chat pool + batch pool for heavy gens.
2) Is the 420 active sessions an absolute concurrent active model KV context size? Need to know session length distribution — median session length and 95th percentile (minutes).
3) Which model family are they expecting to pin to (open Llama-2-70B style or 13B sized models)? The quantization and kernel choices differ a lot.

If we assume: chat SLA p95=350ms target, batch can be p95=2000ms, model = 13B quantized, and typical token mix Jordan provided, preliminary estimate is approx: 6 x A100-like devices for chat (sustained) + 2 x cheaper GPUs for batch. That's a starting sketch; I'll formalize into an XLSX.

I'll email Jordan a short list of follow-ups; Camila, can you confirm if Ava requires session affinity or sticky routing? That changes pool sizing.

- Ben
Ben Carter
Head of Solutions Architecture | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai


From: Camila Reyes <camila.reyes@redwood.ai>
To: Jordan Lee <jordan.lee@avasolutions.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 25 May 2028 15:41:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Dedicated capacity quick-scan: traffic, concurrency & response-lengths

Jordan —

Thanks for the quick turnaround and the telemetry. Two small follow-ups so we can lock assumptions and send the first-pass estimate this afternoon:

1) Session affinity: Are conversations pinned to a single backend for the lifetime (yes/no)? If yes, what's the median session duration (minutes) and the 95th percentile?
2) Model family: Which model are you evaluating? If you haven't decided, mark "13B quantized" and we will run a sensitivity to 70B later.
3) Any compliance constraints that force on-prem or VPC-only? If so, that affects SLA & autoscaling policy recommendations.

If you prefer a 15 min call to close these, Ben and I are available at 4:00pm PT. Otherwise reply inline and we'll proceed.

Camila Reyes
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
camila.reyes@redwood.ai

> - Streaming: ~35% streaming for chat (token-by-token), rest are full-response.
> - Seasonality: Weekly spike Tuesdays 10:00–10:30 PST, monthly spike on first business day.
> 
> Telemetry: attached telemetry_snippet.json (500 lines). Let us know if you need a larger sample.
> 
> Jordan
> Head of ML Engineering | Ava Solutions
> jordan.lee@avasolutions.com
> +1 (617) 555-0199

title: Enterprise access onboarding — mutual action plan & SSO/SCIM kickoff
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Wong <alex.wong@infrabank.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 21 May 2028 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Enterprise access onboarding — mutual action plan & SSO/SCIM kickoff

Hi Alex —

Thanks again for the time today. Per our call, I wanted to share a short mutual action plan and clarify the access-control items we'll need to unblock the pilot. Attached: InfraBank_MutualActionPlan_20280521.pdf (high-level milestones + owners).

Priority items for the first 2 weeks:
- SSO/SAML: Metadata exchange + test IdP; we recommend test bind using the RelayState flow.
- SCIM: Confirm core attributes and whether you want group sync (we usually start with users-only).
- IP allowlist: Provide 2-3 CIDR ranges for your egress so we can whitelist.
- Service accounts & org policies: Define initial service account owners and a list of needed API scopes.

Suggested next steps:
1) Please confirm SSO contact + test IdP endpoint (who at InfraBank will run the test).
2) Share a sample user CSV or SCIM attributes if you have a schema preference (I can share a template).
3) Procurement: let me know the PO/contract timeline so we can align the go-live.

I cc'd Marissa (CS lead) and Priyom (implementation) so we can do a direct handoff once you confirm the SSO contact. If it helps, we can do a 30-min kickoff this week — propose Thu 11:00am PT / Fri 9:00am PT.

Best,
Ben
Ben Carter | Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0191

Attachment: InfraBank_MutualActionPlan_20280521.pdf

-----Original Message-----
From: Alex Wong <alex.wong@infrabank.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Samira Patel <samira.patel@infrabank.com>, Procurement <procurement@infrabank.com>
Date: Mon, 21 May 2028 10:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Enterprise access onboarding — mutual action plan & SSO/SCIM kickoff

Ben —

Thanks for the summary and for putting this together. A few quick answers:
- SSO contact: Samira Patel (cc'd) will run the IdP tests. Her team prefers SAML 2.0 with SHA-256 signing.
- SCIM: we want users + department attribute, group sync is optional for phase 1. I'll attach a sample CSV.
- IP allowlist: we need your outbound IP ranges for our security team; they require CIDR ranges and an expected maintenance window for changes.
- Procurement: timeline is ~2-3 weeks; procurement requested that a final MA + SOW be attached to the DocuSign package.

Can you also confirm whether Redwood supports attribute-based group mapping (role -> group) or if that's something we need to handle from the IdP side?

Happy to take a kickoff Thu 11:00am PT.

Thanks,
Alex
Alex Wong | Director of Identity & Access
InfraBank
alex.wong@infrabank.com | +1 (212) 555-0123

Attachment: infra_sample_users.csv

-----Original Message-----
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Wong <alex.wong@infrabank.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>, Procurement <procurement@infrabank.com>
Date: Tue, 22 May 2028 11:30:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Enterprise access onboarding — mutual action plan & SSO/SCIM kickoff

Alex — thanks, this is perfect. Quick answers inline and attachments below.

> Can you confirm whether Redwood supports attribute-based group mapping (role -> group) or if that's something we need to handle from the IdP side?

We support both patterns: you can either sync groups via SCIM (Redwood will persist group objects and we map them to roles in our RBAC) or you can emit an attribute (e.g., groups or role) from the IdP and Redwood will honor that claim at login. For phase 1, users-only SCIM + an IdP attribute for role is the fastest path.

IP allowlist: our standard outbound CIDR ranges for hosted customers are 34.210.32.0/21 and 35.196.64.0/22. If you have a strict firewall setup we can open a temporary tunnel for the pilot instead (let us know).

SCIM attributes: attached scim-attributes-example.csv with the common attributes we send (id, userName, givenName, familyName, email, department, groups). If you prefer a different attribute name for department we can remap.

Kickoff: Thu 24 May 2028 11:00am PT works for us. I'll schedule a 30-min call and include the implementation checklist.

Procurement: good call — I'll attach the MA and a short SOW and route to DocuSign once you confirm final billing contact and PO.

Implementation notes for the CS/Eng handoff (cc Marissa + Priyom):
- Priyom: lead engineer for SSO/SCIM test, owns runbook and can be on the kickoff.
- Marissa: customer success lead, will coordinate cutover and acceptance.
- Internal checklist attached to the mutual action plan.

Ben
Ben Carter | Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0191

Attachments: scim-attributes-example.csv, InfraBank_MutualActionPlan_20280521.pdf

-----Original Message-----
From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 23 May 2028 08:00:00 -07:00
Subject: Internal: InfraBank handoff & immediate tasks

Folks —

I'll take point on the kickoff cadence and acceptance criteria. Quick internal TODOs before Thu kickoff: 
- Priyom: prepare a short test plan for SAML sign-in (IdP metadata validation + test user flow) and a SCIM bulk-provision runbook.
- Ben: please confirm the DocuSign link once procurement signs off so Marissa can validate billing fields.
- Jordan: will you scope a short run to validate our outbound IPs from staging and flag any additional ranges needed? (CC'd you on this thread.)

I'll join the kickoff and bring our cutover checklist. Keeping pilot scope minimal (users + department) will speed acceptance.

Marissa
Marissa Cole | Head of Customer Success
Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai

-----Original Message-----
From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 24 May 2028 17:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Internal: InfraBank handoff & immediate tasks

- I created an implementation ticket (JIRA: RED-1129) with the SAML/SCIM test plan and assigned tasks for the pilot.
- Uploaded scim-attributes-example.csv to the InfraBank folder on our drive and linked the MA.
- I'll be on Thu 11:00am PT kickoff and can run the IdP metadata exchange during the call.

Ben/Marissa — I'll follow up after the call with test results and next steps.

Thanks,
Priyom Das | Implementation Lead
